Each valid target must roll CON opposed by your Magic (Necromancy). On a failure, the target takes 1d6 Veil damage and you restore 2 HP. If no creature fails, you are reduced to 0 HP normally and the Strain is still spent.
career_gravesinger_hold_the_body_rules-u: Type: Passive (Thrall Enhancement) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — A raised corpse under your command that would be destroyed by damage may instead make a CON roll at Hard difficulty — on a success, it remains active at 1 HP rather than collapsing. This may only occur once per raised corpse.
career_gravesinger_life_siphon_rules-u: Type: Action (Drain/Transfer) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Target one living creature within Short range. Spirits, drones, constructs, and non-living entities cannot be targeted. Roll Magic (Necromancy) opposed by the target's CON. On a success, deal 1d8 Veil damage to the target and restore HP equal to half the damage dealt (rounded down) to either yourself or one ally within Short range. The recipient may not exceed their maximum HP.
career_gravesinger_pale_dominion_rules-u: Type: Action (Field) • Cost: 2d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Establish a Medium-radius field of necrotic dominance centered on you for the remainder of the Scene. Maintaining this field is a concentration effect — if you initiate any other ability that requires concentration, the field immediately collapses. While inside the field, all hostile living creatures suffer 1 penalty die on CON rolls and cannot benefit from passive HP recovery of any kind. Spirits, drones, constructs, and non-living entities are unaffected by the field. At the start of each hostile living creature's turn while inside the field, that creature must roll CON opposed by your Magic (Necromancy). On a failure, it takes 1d4 Veil damage. You and up to four designated allies are unaffected. If you are incapacitated or Stunned, the field collapses immediately. While Pale Dominion is active, you cannot use Entropy Aura or Death Curse — the field subsumes those effects.
career_gravesinger_pale_resonance_rules-: Type: Free Action (Setup) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Before casting a Necromancy spell, gain +1 bonus die on the spellcasting roll. This bonus applies to the next qualifying Necromancy spell cast this Scene and expires if unused by Scene end.
career_gravesinger_pale_servant_rules-u: Type: Action (Sentient Thrall) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Target the corpse of a sentient being within Short range. Roll Magic (Necromancy). On a success, the corpse rises under your command for the remainder of the Scene. Apply the corpse's condition (Fresh or Decayed) to determine its physical capabilities — see Corpse Condition. The Pale Servant thinks, remembers, and reasons using its original mind and knowledge. It obeys your commands without resistance — it will fight, move, perform tasks, and act as directed. However, obedience does not extend to compelled disclosure. A Pale Servant is not forced to reveal information, answer questions, or share what it knows. A servant that was hostile to you or your allies in life retains that hostility in death — it will follow orders, but will not volunteer information and may refuse to answer entirely. A servant may choose to speak freely, but that choice belongs to it. Persuasion, leverage, or the right approach may still open that door. A Fresh corpse recalls details with full precision. If the corpse is Decayed, its memory may be incomplete or imprecise — exact phrasing, recent sensory detail, and rapidly changing information such as codes or current operational data may be degraded or missing. Stable knowledge — names, locations, relationships, procedures — remains intact. It acts on your Initiative and retains all skills and abilities permitted by its condition. When the Scene ends, the servant collapses permanently and cannot be raised again. Only one raised corpse may be active at a time — see Pale Sovereignty.
career_gravesinger_pale_shroud_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Defense) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Wrap yourself in a layer of death-attuned Veil energy. Until the start of your next turn, you gain +2 Veil Soak. Additionally, the first creature that strikes you in melee before this effect ends must roll CON opposed by your Magic (Necromancy) or become Shaken until the end of its next turn.
career_gravesinger_pale_sight_rules-u: Type: Action (Attune) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: At-will — While you have an active raised corpse under your command, spend your Action to attune your senses to the corpse's perception. Until you end this effect as a Free Action or the corpse collapses, you see and hear through the corpse's senses rather than your own. While attuned, you cannot take Actions — only Maneuvers — and you suffer 1 penalty die on all Reactions as your awareness of your own surroundings is reduced. The corpse continues to follow your last issued command while you are attuned. You may issue a new command as a Free Action, which does not break attunement.
career_gravesinger_raise_the_dead_rules-: Type: Action (Thrall) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Target one non-sentient corpse — an animal, a small creature, vermin — within Short range. Roll Magic (Necromancy). On a success, the corpse rises under your command for the remainder of the Scene. Apply the corpse's condition (Fresh or Decayed) to determine its capabilities — see Corpse Condition. The raised creature acts on your Initiative and obeys your commands completely. It cannot use complex behaviors or special abilities. Issuing a new order to the raised creature requires a Maneuver. Without a new order it continues following its last issued command. It collapses at Scene end or if concentration is broken — see Pale Sovereignty. Only one raised corpse may be active at a time — see Pale Sovereignty.
career_gravesinger_soul_harvest_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Escalating) • Cost: 2d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session activation — Declare at the start of your first turn in a hostile scene. Until the end of the Scene, whenever any creature is reduced to 0 HP within Medium range — ally, enemy, or raised thrall — you immediately restore 1d6 HP and gain +10% to your next Magic (Necromancy) roll this Scene. This bonus stacks to a maximum of +20%. HP restored by this talent counts toward Healing Saturation and daily healing limits — the Gravesinger may choose to decline the HP restoration to avoid triggering saturation. Additionally, the first time this effect triggers each Scene, you may immediately use Raise the Dead on the fallen creature as a Free Action without spending Strain, provided it qualifies as a non-sentient corpse.
career_gravesinger_soul_rend_rules-u: Type: Action (Execute) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Target one living creature within Short range. Spirits, drones, constructs, and non-living entities cannot be targeted. Roll Magic (Necromancy) opposed by the target's CON. On a success, deal 2d8 Veil damage. On a failure, deal 1d6 Veil damage only and the Strain is still spent.
career_gravesinger_strain_buffer_rules-u: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum Strain by +1. (This talent may appear in multiple Career Trees.)
career_gravesinger_the_last_word_rules-u: Type: Action (Terminal Curse) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Target one living creature within Short range. Spirits, drones, constructs, and non-living entities cannot be targeted. Roll Magic (Necromancy) opposed by the target's POW. On a success, the target is cursed for the remainder of the Scene. Maintaining this curse is a concentration effect — if you initiate any other ability that requires concentration, the curse immediately ends. At the start of each of the target's turns while cursed, the target rolls CON opposed by your Magic (Necromancy). On a failure, the target takes Veil damage that bypasses all Veil Soak — armor and resistances provide no protection against it — starting at 1d4, escalating one die step on each subsequent failure: 1d4 → 1d6 → 1d8, to a maximum of 1d8. On a success, the target takes no damage that round but the curse persists. On a failure of the initial activation roll, the target instead takes 1d4 Veil damage once and is Shaken until the end of its next turn. This effect ends immediately if you are incapacitated.
career_gravesinger_vitality_tap_rules-u: Type: Action (Drain) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Target one living creature within Short range. Spirits, drones, constructs, and non-living entities cannot be targeted. Roll Magic (Necromancy) opposed by the target's CON. On a success, deal 1d6 Veil damage to the target and restore 1d4 HP to yourself only. This self-healing does not count toward Healing Saturation or daily healing limits. On a failure, the effect does not resolve and the Strain is still spent.
career_gravesinger_wither_rules-u: Type: Action (Debuff) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Target one creature within Short range. Roll Magic (Necromancy) opposed by the target's CON. On a success, until the start of your next turn, the target suffers 1 penalty die on all physical Actions. On a failure, the effect does not resolve and the Strain is still spent.
career_hexbreaker_break_the_pattern_rule: Type: Action (Disruption) • Cost: 1d6 + 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — You slam your Bound Focus into the flow of the fight, fracturing timing and coordination. Choose one opponent you are Engaged with. Until the end of the current Round, all hostile opponents within Short range of that opponent suffer the following effects: they cannot benefit from Assist actions; they suffer 1 penalty die on the first Reaction they attempt this Round; and any opponent that moves, disengages, or changes targets during this Round suffers –10% to their next roll before the end of their next turn. After this effect ends, you are Shaken until the start of your next turn.
career_hexbreaker_breakers_reversal_rule: Type: Free Action (Rider) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You successfully land a Fight Back against an opponent Engaged with you using your Bound Focus. After resolving the Fight Back normally, the target suffers +1d6 physical damage and is Impaired until the start of its next turn. This additional damage is not a separate attack, cannot be opposed, and does not trigger reactions.
career_hexbreaker_claim_the_exchange_rul: Type: Free Action (Commitment) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: At-will — At the start of your turn, while you are Engaged with an opponent, you may commit to the exchange. Until the start of your next turn, the first time that opponent makes a melee or unarmed attack against you, you gain +10% on any Fight Back reaction made against that attack. If you disengage before this triggers, the effect ends with no benefit.
career_hexbreaker_close_in_guard_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Guard) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: An opponent within Short range that is not Engaged with you targets an ally with an attack. Impose 1 penalty die on the triggering attack. If the attack misses, you may immediately Shift to become Engaged with the attacker, provided you can reach them. This movement does not trigger reactions.
career_hexbreaker_crack_the_timing_rules: Type: Maneuver (Hex) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose an opponent Engaged with you. Until the start of your next turn, the first Reaction that opponent attempts automatically suffers 1 penalty die. If that Reaction fails, it is still spent.
career_hexbreaker_disruptive_grip_rules-: Type: Reaction (Interrupt) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: An opponent Engaged with you attempts to disengage, reposition, or use a movement ability. Roll your Melee Weapons or Unarmed Combat opposed by the opponent’s Dodge or Athletics. On a success, the movement occurs but the target suffers 1 penalty die on its next roll before the end of its turn. On a Hard Success, the movement fails and the target remains Engaged.
career_hexbreaker_edge_of_attrition_rule: Type: Action (Stance) • Cost: 1d6 Strain to enter; 1 Strain per Round to maintain • Usage: 1/Session — You enter a duelist’s stance against an opponent you are Engaged with. Choose one opponent you are Engaged with; the stance lasts while you remain Engaged with that opponent and continue to pay the upkeep cost. While active: (1) You gain +10% on all Fight Back reactions against the chosen opponent. (2) The chosen opponent suffers –10% to all melee or unarmed attacks against targets other than you. (3) The first time each Round you and the chosen opponent both succeed on opposed melee checks against each other, your success tier is treated as one step higher. This stance does not stack with other stance-based, zone-based, or threat-enforcing effects. The stance ends immediately if you disengage, lose Engaged status, fail to pay upkeep, or choose a new target. When the stance ends, you are Impaired until the start of your next turn.
career_hexbreaker_faultline_strike_rules: Type: Free Action (Hex) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you hit an opponent Engaged with you using your Bound Focus, you may declare Faultline Strike. After damage is resolved, choose one of the following effects to apply to the target until the start of its next turn: –10% to all attacks, –10% to all defenses, or 1 penalty die on its next Reaction. This effect represents structural failure in the target’s stance, wards, or systems and does not stack with itself.
career_hexbreaker_final_rend_rules-u: Type: Action (Execution) • Cost: 1d6 + 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — You must be Engaged with an opponent you have already hit this Scene using your Bound Focus. Make a single melee attack with your Bound Focus against that opponent. On a hit, the attack deals arcane damage and gains +2d6 damage. Damage is reduced only by the opponent’s arcane damage reduction. If this attack reduces the opponent to half HP or lower, they are Staggered until the end of their next turn. After resolving this attack, you lose all Reactions and suffer 1 penalty die on all defenses until the start of your next turn. This attack cannot benefit from other Hex or Rider effects.
career_hexbreaker_fracture_edge_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Hex) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: This strike only — When you hit with a melee attack using your Bound Focus, you may reduce the damage dealt by 2. If you do, the target suffers –10% to its next attack or defense roll before the end of its next turn. If the target relies on active wards, reactive shielding, or similar protections, apply the penalty as –20% instead.
career_hexbreaker_lockstep_advance_rules: Type: Free Action (Bind) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: This exchange only — When you enter Engaged range with a target using your Bound Focus, you may declare Lockstep Advance. Until the end of the current exchange, neither you nor the target may disengage without spending an Action. If either of you disengages this way, the other may immediately Shift one range band to follow.
career_hexbreaker_marked_by_contact_rule: Type: Free Action (Bind) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you hit an opponent with a melee attack using your Bound Focus, you may Mark that target until the start of your next turn. While Marked, the target suffers –10% to all attacks and defenses against opponents other than you. This Mark ends immediately if you disengage.
career_hexbreaker_oath_of_the_breach_rul: Type: Action (Vow) • Cost: 1d6 + 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose one opponent you can see and name them as your breach. Until the end of the Scene, while you and the chosen opponent are within Medium range of each other, the following effects apply: (1) The first time each Round the chosen opponent attacks, disengages, or targets someone other than you, they immediately become Shaken. (2) The first time each Round you hit the chosen opponent with a melee attack using your Bound Focus, you may either clear Shaken from yourself or gain +10% on your next melee or Fight Back roll against that opponent before the end of your next turn. This effect ends early if you become Incapacitated, voluntarily retreat beyond Medium range, or choose to end the vow as a Free Action.
career_hexbreaker_press_the_fault_rules-: Type: Maneuver (Hex) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose a target Engaged with you while using your Bound Focus. Until the start of your next turn, the first time that target would gain a bonus die, ignore a penalty die, or receive a defensive benefit from an active protection effect, it instead suffers 1 penalty die on that roll. Active protection effects are limited to abilities or effects that explicitly grant bonus dice, negate penalty dice, provide reaction-based defense, or apply conditional damage reduction. Passive armor, static soak, and always-on equipment do not qualify. This talent does not remove effects and applies only once.
career_hexbreaker_punishing_focus_rules-: Type: Passive • Usage: At-will — While you are Engaged with an opponent using your Bound Focus, that opponent suffers –10% to all attack rolls that do not target you. This penalty ends immediately if you are no longer Engaged with that opponent. This effect does not stack with other targeting or threat-based penalties.
career_hexbreaker_rend_the_ward_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Hex) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: This strike only — When you hit with a melee attack using your Bound Focus, you may declare Rend the Ward. Until the end of the current Round, the target cannot benefit from arcane wards, reactive shielding, or effects that grant bonus dice to defense against attacks made by you. This does not dispel effects; it suppresses their benefit against your attacks only.
career_hexbreaker_shatter_the_seal_rules: Type: Free Action (Hex) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: This strike only — When you hit an opponent with a melee attack using your Bound Focus, you may declare Shatter the Seal. For this attack, reduce the target’s total physical damage reduction from armor, wards, and similar protective effects by 2, to a minimum of the target’s base physical soak. If the target relied on arcane protection or reactive shielding, they also suffer 1 penalty die on their next defense roll before the end of their next turn. This effect applies only to this attack and does not dispel or suppress ongoing effects.
career_hexbreaker_stand_the_line_rules-u: Type: Action (Stance) • Cost: 1d6 Strain to enter; 1 Strain per Round to maintain • Usage: 1/Session — You plant yourself and dare the fight to break on you. Until the stance ends, you project a Short-radius threat zone centered on you. While active: (1) Hostile opponents within the zone suffer –10% to all attacks against targets other than you. (2) The first time each Round an opponent within the zone attacks a target other than you, you may immediately take a Free Action to move one range band toward that opponent (ending Engaged if applicable). (3) The first time each Round you are hit by an attack while this stance is active, reduce the damage by 1d6 (after DR). This stance does not stack with other stance-based, zone-based, or threat-enforcing effects. The stance ends immediately if you are moved unwillingly, become Incapacitated, or fail to pay the upkeep cost. When the stance ends, you are Shaken until the start of your next turn.
career_hexbreaker_stay_the_blade_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Guard) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: An opponent Engaged with you hits you with a melee attack. Reduce the damage by 2, then impose –10% on that opponent’s next attack roll before the end of its next turn. If the triggering attack relied on a bonus die, the penalty is instead –20%.
career_hexbreaker_turn_the_guard_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Counter) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: An opponent Engaged with you successfully Dodges or Parries your melee attack. Immediately impose –10% on that opponent’s next attack roll before the end of its next turn. If the defense relied on a Reaction, that opponent also suffers 1 penalty die on its next Reaction before the end of its next turn.
career_icon_all_eyes_on_me_rules-u: Type: Action (Compel) • Cost: 3 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Seize the emotional spotlight. All NPCs within Medium who can see or hear you must roll POW. On a failure, they’re compelled to direct their next verbal or physical action toward you if applicable, or suffer Impaired for 1 round. Hostile combatants gain +1 bonus die to resist.
career_icon_burn_it_brighter_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Defiant Surge) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: During a high-stakes moment (public confrontation, broadcast moment, crisis, rally, or active threat scene), when you or an ally within Short fails an Action that was affected by one or more penalty dice. Effect: The triggering roll is immediately rerolled using the same dice pool, including all original bonus and penalty dice. The new result replaces the original.
career_icon_burning_signature_rules-u: Type: Passive (Fame) • Usage: 1/Scene — The first time each scene you leverage your recognized persona, voice, or work against a character who has encountered you before, gain +20% to that social roll. This effect lasts only for the triggering roll.
career_icon_command_the_stage_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Performance Setup) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you perform before a group (live or broadcast), choose one for the rest of the scene: gain +1 bonus die to Charm, Persuade, or Leadership; or the next hostile social roll made by the audience suffers 1 penalty die.
career_icon_crowd_momentum_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Carryover) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After a successful Perform that captures audience attention, gain +1 bonus die on your next Persuade, Charm, or Leadership roll this scene.
career_icon_crowdcarver_rules-u: Type: Action (Field Tone) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Declare an emotional tone (e.g., fear, reverence, defiance, serenity) for the scene involving a group. That group adopts the tone for the remainder of the scene unless hostile individuals pass POW. This doesn’t compel behavior but tilts reactions to persuasion, fear, and influence.
career_icon_cultural_gravity_rules-u: Type: Passive (Downtime) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Gain +20% to downtime performance rolls to earn credits, build audiences, or influence venues. Applies to DreamFeed contracts, underground showcases, or professional bookings.
career_icon_echo_beyond_the_feed_rules-u: Type: Action (Invoke Memory) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After a scene in which you performed or left a strong impression, automatically succeed on one Charm or Persuade roll (not Deception) against a witness (live or via feed). This cannot compel actions that would be fundamentally against the target’s nature, loyalties, or immediate survival instincts.
career_icon_emotional_contagion_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (Shift Attitude) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you successfully sway an audience, one NPC observer shifts from Neutral → Friendly for the rest of the scene.
career_icon_encore_effect_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (Reroll/Boost) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — After you fail a Perform check, reroll it. If the reroll succeeds, grant +10% to the next roll of one ally or NPC who observed it this scene.
career_icon_encore_of_will_rules-u: Type: Action (Cleanse) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Remove Panicked and Suppressed from allies within Medium who can see or hear you.
career_icon_hold_the_line_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Rally Under Fire) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you or an ally within Short drops to 50% HP or less during a hostile scene, you seize the moment to keep morale from breaking. Choose one ally who can see or hear you. That ally may immediately ignore one penalty die on their next Action this round that directly responds to the threat that caused the trigger.
career_icon_iconic_rally_rules-u: Type: Action (Rally) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — All allies within Medium who can see or hear you instantly recover 1d4 Strain.
career_icon_living_legend_rules-u: Type: Action (Narrative Authority) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you enter a social or public scene involving individuals or groups familiar with your reputation, declare Living Legend. Until the end of the scene, hostile social rolls made against you suffer 1 penalty die, and allies gain +10% on social rolls that directly invoke your legacy, name, or prior deeds. This does not compel behavior or override loyalties.
career_icon_neurolinked_presence_rules-u: Type: Passive (Feed) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — While performing through a DreamFeed/emotion-sync venue, gain +10% to Perform or Charm for the scene. Applies to both live and recorded feeds.
career_icon_pulse_of_the_crowd_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Recover) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — After a successful Perform check, regain 1d4 Strain.
career_icon_resonant_persona_rules-u: Type: Passive (Persona) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Create a named performance persona (look/theme/emotional arc). While performing in-character, gain +10% to Perform, Charm, or Persuade. Only one active Persona at a time; may be taken again to maintain an additional Persona.
career_icon_shared_spotlight_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Closer) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Deliver a closing performance/rally at scene end. Allies who witnessed it recover an additional 1d4 Strain during their next short rest.
career_icon_showstopper_rules-u: Type: Passive (Substitution) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When initiating a performance or seizing attention, you may roll Perform instead of Deception or Intimidate to distract, misdirect, or engage a crowd.
career_icon_signature_crescendo_rules-u: Type: Action (Climax) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Transform a Perform or Persuade roll into a defining moment. On a success, all targets influenced by it become more receptive: you gain +10% to all further social rolls with them for the rest of the scene. In addition, choose one NPC or group affected; their attitude toward you cannot worsen for the remainder of the session unless you betray or directly harm them.
career_icon_stagecraft_rules-u: Type: Passive (Downtime) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Gain +10% to downtime performance rolls to earn credits or build influence. May also apply to artistic Networking scenes at GM discretion.
career_icon_strain_buffer_rules-u: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum Strain by +1. (This talent may appear in multiple Career Trees.)
career_icon_street_stage_discipline_rule: Type: Passive (Grit) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Ignore 1 penalty die to Perform when performing in chaotic/crowded/dangerous environments (combat zones, underground clubs, protests, etc.).
career_icon_this_moment_will_be_remember: Type: Passive (Narrative Weight) • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Once per session, when a scene ends in which you played a visible role, you may declare this talent. Choose one significant NPC, faction, or audience present. For future scenes in which that NPC, faction, or audience is directly involved, the first roll you make to influence, rally, or sway them gains 1 bonus die, as the memory of this moment lingers.
career_icon_voice_of_the_moment_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Rally) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — During a high-stakes moment (negotiation, crisis, rally), grant +10% to all allies’ next social roll this scene.
career_investigator_absolute_reconstruct: Type: Action • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After concentrating for one full Round at a location or over a dataset you have examined this Scene, roll Investigation; on a success, ask the GM up to three specific questions about the sequence of the most recent significant event that occurred there within the last hour, with answers limited to what can be inferred from physical, digital, or arcane evidence and without revealing hidden intent. Until the end of the Scene, the first allied Action that directly exploits one of these answers gains +1 bonus die.
career_investigator_anchor_the_moment_ru: Type: Reaction • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When an ally within Short range fails a Perception, Insight, or Investigation check, you may spend 1 Strain to allow them to reroll the check and take the new result.
career_investigator_anticipated_response: Type: Reaction • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — After a creature you have used Focused Subject on declares an action, you may spend 1 Strain to gain +1 bonus die on your next roll made directly in response to that action this Round.
career_investigator_broad_spectrum_asses: Type: Action • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — For the remainder of the Scene, gain +1 bonus die on Investigation and Perception checks; this does not negate penalty dice and does not apply to combat attacks.
career_investigator_casefile_momentum_ru: Type: Passive (Momentum) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — The first time each scene that you succeed on an Investigation, Insight, or Perception check that reveals a meaningful lead, contradiction, or vulnerability, choose one: gain +1 bonus die on your next Action that directly follows from that discovery or allow one ally who can hear you to gain +1 bonus die on their next Action that exploits it.
career_investigator_clean_execution_rule: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When benefiting from Methodical Approach or Deliberate Follow-Up, you may also remove one complication or drawback imposed by the GM on a Success (not a failure) related to that investigation.
career_investigator_cold_case_instinct_r: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Once per Session, when a Scene ends without resolving a central question you were actively investigating, you retain one unresolved lead; the next time that lead becomes relevant, gain +1 bonus die on the first Investigation, Insight, or Interrogation check made to pursue it.
career_investigator_crack_the_mask_rules: Type: Action • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — During a social interaction, after succeeding on Exploit Tension, roll Insight opposed by the target’s Deception or Willpower. On a success, the target cannot take bonus dice on social rolls against you for the remainder of the Scene.
career_investigator_decisive_leverage_ru: Type: Action • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — During a social confrontation, after succeeding on an opposed social roll against a target you have engaged earlier this Scene, choose up to three allies who can see or hear the exchange. The next opposed social roll made by each chosen ally against that target this Scene gains +1 bonus die. This does not compel behavior or force truthful answers.
career_investigator_deliberate_follow_up: Type: Reaction • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you fail an Investigation or Perception check that benefited from Methodical Approach, you may spend 1 Strain to ask the GM one narrowly scoped follow-up question about what your attempt almost revealed; the answer may be partial or uncertain and does not convert the failure into a success.
career_investigator_exploit_tension_rule: Type: Action • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — During a social interaction, after succeeding with Pressure Point, spend 1 Strain to impose one penalty die on the target’s next social roll this Scene that directly opposes you.
career_investigator_focused_subject_rule: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After successfully using Read the Room on a target, your next non-damaging action against that target this Scene (including Setups, Maneuvers, and Social Actions) gains +1 bonus die.
career_investigator_initial_read_rules-u: Type: Action • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After entering a new scene or environment, make an Investigation or Perception check. On a success, the GM reveals one notable inconsistency, omission, or detail that does not fit the apparent situation.
career_investigator_methodical_approach_: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you take at least one full Round to examine evidence, a location, or records before acting, ignore one penalty die on your next Investigation or Perception check related to that subject.
career_investigator_operational_override: Type: Reaction • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When a Scene pivots due to a revealed clue, contradiction, or discovery you directly uncovered this Scene, you may immediately allow yourself and up to two allies within Close range to each take one Maneuver outside the normal turn order. These Maneuvers cannot be used to attack or cast spells and must be resolved immediately or the opportunity is lost.
career_investigator_poised_under_fire_ru: Type: Reaction • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you would be Surprised, you may spend 2 Strain to act normally during the first Round, but you cannot make Reactions until after your first turn.
career_investigator_preemptive_positioni: Type: Reaction • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When combat begins, you may spend 2 Strain to either move one range increment or gain +1 bonus die on your first Dodge or Fight Back Reaction this Scene.
career_investigator_pressure_point_rules: Type: Action • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — During a social interaction, make an opposed Insight vs. Deception or Persuade. On a success, your next social check against the target this Scene gains +1 bonus die.
career_investigator_read_the_room_rules-: Type: Action • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose one individual you can observe. Gain +20% Insight on your next check targeting that individual this Scene.
career_investigator_ready_before_it_brea: Type: Reaction • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When Situational Awareness applies, you may also draw a weapon or take cover as part of the same Reaction.
career_investigator_reconstructive_pass_: Type: Action • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After examining a location, object, or records for one full Round, roll Investigation. On a success, ask the GM one specific question about the most recent significant action taken there. The answer must be grounded in available evidence, not hidden intent.
career_investigator_resonance_trace_rule: Type: Action • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — After successfully using Initial Read in a scene involving magic or Veil effects, gain +20% Arcana for the remainder of the Scene to identify magical signatures, disturbances, or their general direction of origin.
career_investigator_situational_awarenes: Type: Reaction • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When initiative is rolled and you are not Surprised, spend 1 Strain to gain +10% on your Initiative roll.
career_investigator_temporal_intuition_r: Type: Action • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Roll Insight. On a success, the GM describes one major event likely to occur within the next hour if no meaningful intervention takes place, framed as a probable outcome rather than a certainty.
career_investigator_veil_discontinuity_s: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you encounter magical or Veil-based deception, illusions, or obfuscation, you may roll Insight or Arcana; on a success, you recognize the presence of falsification and gain +20% on your next action taken to analyze or counter its source.
career_marksman_advanced_spotter_doctrin: Type: Maneuver (Target Designation) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you successfully designate a target with Spotter’s Mark, you may escalate the designation. Until the start of your n
career_marksman_aim_lock_rules-u: Type: Passive (when disrupted) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — If an effect would cause you to lose the benefits of a prepared or uninterrupted aimed shot due to a Dodge or minor forced movement, you may spend 2 Strain to maintain those benefits into your next turn. Major forced movement still disrupts the shot.
career_marksman_braced_position_rules-u: Type: Maneuver • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Become Braced. While Braced, Single Shot rifle attacks gain +10%. Moving breaks Braced. You may only benefit from either Braced Position or Steady Breath on a given attack.
career_marksman_cold_bore_rules-u: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Your first rifle attack each Scene that follows Quick Aim or Focused Aim gains +1 damage on a hit.
career_marksman_counter_sniper_drill_rul: Type: Reaction • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You are targeted by a ranged attack from Long or Extreme range. Effect: Make a Perception roll opposed by the attacker’s Stealth. On a success, you pinpoint their position and gain +10% to your next rifle attack against that attacker before the end of your next turn.
career_marksman_counter_sniper_protocol_: Type: Reaction • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You are hit by a ranged attack from an unseen attacker whose position is known through observation, sensors, drones, or ally callouts. Effect: Immediately make a Single Shot rifle attack at –20% as a Reaction against that attacker before damage is applied.
career_marksman_dead_line_rules-u: Type: Special post-shot • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After an uninterrupted, carefully prepared shot, choose two visible enemies within your front arc who are within one range band of each other and at Medium range or farther. Make one Single Shot rifle attack at –10%; on a hit, both targets take normal weapon damage (roll once, apply to both). This attack cannot impale.
career_marksman_ghillie_mastery_rules-u: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — While you remain stationary and have not attacked this round, attempts to locate you at Medium or longer range suffer 1 penalty die. You gain +10% Stealth while observing or repositioning before you shoot. These benefits end immediately after you attack or are successfully detected.
career_marksman_ghost_window_rules-u: Type: Reaction • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: An enemy you can see declares movement or an attack that would break line of sight, change range bands, or reach cover. Effect: Make an immediate Single Shot rifle attack before the triggering action resolves. If the attack hits, damage is resolved normally and the triggering movement or repositioning is halted.
career_marksman_glint_trap_rules-u: Type: Reaction • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: A ranged attacker at Long or Extreme range misses you. Effect: That attacker’s next attack against you this Scene suffers 1 penalty die.
career_marksman_impossible_shot_rules-u: Type: Attack • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After an uninterrupted, carefully prepared shot, you may attempt a Single Shot rifle attack at a target up to two range bands beyond your weapon’s listed maximum (never beyond Extreme). Apply normal range modifiers plus one penalty die per band beyond maximum. On a hit, the attack automatically impales. On a fumble, the shot fails and your position is revealed.
career_marksman_kill_chain_rules-u: Type: Special immediate • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — After you hit with a rifle attack that followed Focused Aim, make one additional Single Shot rifle attack at a different target within Short of the original target’s position at –10%. This follow-up does not benefit from Focused Aim, cannot impale regardless of success level, and overrides the Single Shot once-per-turn limit only for this attack.
career_marksman_kill_confirmed_rules-u: Type: Passive (after hit) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After you resolve a successful Single Shot rifle hit, you may upgrade the result to a critical (impale). If the hit was already a Hard Success or better, instead grant the attack Armor Piercing 4. This benefit cannot be used if the attack was made as a Reaction.
career_marksman_lockdown_shot_rules-u: Type: Attack modifier • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare before rolling a Single Shot rifle attack. On a hit, you may reduce the attack’s damage by 1d6 to inflict Hobbled (movement halved) on the target until the start of their next turn.
career_marksman_measured_distance_rules-: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When making a rifle attack at Medium or Long range and you have not moved this turn, ignore 1 penalty die imposed by environmental factors such as poor lighting, glare, light obstruction, or unstable footing. This does not ignore penalties from cover, range, or target abilities.
career_marksman_one_shot_doctrine_rules-: Type: Passive (on hit) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When a Single Shot rifle attack hits with a Hard Success or better, that attack gains Armor Piercing 4 for damage resolution.
career_marksman_overwatch_architect_rule: Type: Action (Set) + Reaction (Interrupts) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — While stationary, establish Overwatch covering your front arc out to your weapon’s maximum range until the start of your next turn. Any enemy that becomes visible in that arc must immediately test POW for Suppression. While Overwatch is active, you may make up to two interrupt Single Shot rifle attacks as Reactions against Suppressed enemies that move or expose themselves (resolved at 0% and –10%). You cannot take other Reactions while Overwatch is active.
career_marksman_quiet_reload_rules-u: Type: Free Action • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — If you have not moved this round and are not Engaged, you may reload a rifle as a Free Action instead of a Maneuver. This does not allow movement or additional Actions as part of the reload.
career_marksman_range_gate_rules-u: Type: Declare before attack • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Treat the target as one range band closer for range penalties (this cannot enable out-of-range shots and does not stack with other effects that treat a target as closer).
career_marksman_relocation_drill_rules-u: Type: Free Action (after a rifle attack following an Aim action resolves) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Immediately take a Move Maneuver to shift one range band. This movement does not grant hiding, does not ignore Disengage, and cannot be used while Engaged.
career_marksman_ricochet_geometry_rules-: Type: Attack • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare before rolling a Single Shot rifle attack when a plausible hard surface or angle is present. Make the attack at –10% and 1 penalty die; ignore all cover for this shot (line of sight is not blocked). Range modifiers still apply. On a fumble, the shot fails and your position is revealed.
career_marksman_sidearm_bailout_rules-u: Type: Reaction • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When an enemy enters or begins Engaged with you, you may immediately draw and fire one handgun attack at them as a Reaction with 1 penalty die. This attack gains no Aim benefits and cannot trigger critical or impale effects. Overrides normal draw timing.
career_marksman_steady_breath_rules-u: Type: Passive (after Quick Aim) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Your next rifle attack that benefits from any Aim gains +10%. If the attack benefits from Focused Aim, this +10% stacks with Focused Aim’s +10% for +20% total (and you still gain Focused Aim’s bonus die). You may only benefit from either Steady Breath or Braced Position on a given attack.
career_marksman_through_and_through_rule: Type: Special immediate • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you hit with a Single Shot rifle attack, if the target is incapacitated or killed, immediately make a second Single Shot rifle attack at a different enemy within Short of the original target’s position at –10%. This follow-up gains Armor Piercing 2 and overrides the Single Shot once-per-turn limit only for this attack.
career_marksman_windcall_rules-u: Type: Passive (declare before a Focused Aim shot) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose one for that attack: remove the penalty die imposed by partial cover, or treat the target as one range band closer for range penalties (cannot exceed the weapon’s maximum band). This effect does not stack with any other ability that treats a target as closer on the same attack. This effect represents a momentary ballistic read and does not enable shots beyond the weapon’s normal capabilities.
career_mindbender_cascade_failure_rules-: Type: Action (Lockdown) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose up to two creatures within Short range that can see or hear you. Roll Magic (Enchantment) opposed by each target's POW. On a success, until the start of your next turn, the target cannot take Reactions and must choose either its Action or its movement on its turn — not both. This does not prevent the use of Maneuvers.
career_mindbender_ego_death_rules-u: Type: Action (Shatter) • Cost: 3 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Target one creature within Short range. Roll Magic (Enchantment) opposed by the target's POW. On a success, deal 1d8 Veil damage and the target loses its Reaction until the end of the Scene. On a failure, the target is unaffected.
career_mindbender_emotional_contagion_ru: Type: Free Action (Spread) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: A creature within Short range becomes Shaken, Frightened, or Disoriented as a result of one of your Mindbender talents or Enchantment spells. Effect: Choose one additional hostile creature within Short range of the affected target. That creature must roll POW opposed by your Magic (Enchantment) or suffer the same condition until the end of its next turn. This spread can only occur once per triggering effect.
career_mindbender_emotional_surge_rules-: Type: Free Action (Rider) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare when you deal Veil damage with a Mindbender talent or Enchantment spell. The target becomes Shaken until the end of its next turn. This does not require a separate roll.
career_mindbender_false_allegiance_rules: Type: Action (Deceive) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Target one creature within Short range. Roll Magic (Enchantment) opposed by the target's POW. On a success, until the start of your next turn, the target treats one creature of your choice as a trusted ally. The target cannot attack or use hostile abilities against that creature and does not count it as an enemy for the purpose of positioning, threat assessment, or triggered Reactions. This does not compel the target to protect or assist the designated creature. After the effect ends, the target is aware something influenced its judgment.
career_mindbender_fractured_reality_rule: Type: Action (Mind Break) • Cost: 2d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose one creature within Short range. Roll Magic (Enchantment) opposed by the target's POW. On a success, until the end of the Scene, at the start of each of the target's turns it must succeed on a POW roll or its Action targets a random creature within range, determined by the GM. This can affect allies and enemies alike. On a failure, the target is unaffected.
career_mindbender_inevitable_choice_rule: Type: Action (Dominate) • Cost: 2d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose one creature within Short range and roll Magic (Enchantment) opposed by the target's POW. On a success, you take direct control of the target until the end of its next turn, choosing its movement, Action, and Maneuvers as if it were acting willingly. You may not force the use of once-per-session abilities, consumables, or self-destructive actions. When control ends, the target is fully aware its will was overridden. After this effect ends, you cannot use Enchantment spells or Mindbender talents that influence thoughts or emotions until the end of your next turn, and you suffer 1 penalty die on all social rolls for the remainder of the Scene.
career_mindbender_memory_fog_rules-u: Type: Action (Suppress) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Target one creature within Short range. Roll Magic (Enchantment) opposed by the target's POW. On a success, choose one talent, spell, or trained skill the target possesses — you must be aware it possesses this capability, whether through observation, Thought Leech, or prior experience. The target cannot use that ability until the end of the Scene. On a failure, the target is instead Disoriented until the end of its next turn.
career_mindbender_mental_ward_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Protect) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: An ally within Short range is targeted by an Enchantment spell, fear effect, Intimidation, or any ability that targets POW or mental resistance. Effect: That ally gains +1 bonus die to resist.
career_mindbender_mind_bastion_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Field) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Project a Short-radius field of psychic resistance centered on you until the start of your next turn. While inside the field, allies gain +1 bonus die on all rolls to resist Enchantment spells, fear effects, Intimidation, and mental conditions. The first hostile Enchantment spell or mind-affecting ability targeting a creature inside the field this round suffers 1 penalty die.
career_mindbender_overwhelming_urge_rule: Type: Action (Mass Compulsion) • Cost: 2d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose one simple directive — such as fight, flee, hold position, pursue, or protect a specific target — and any number of hostile creatures within Medium range that can see or hear you. Each target rolls POW to resist. On a failure, the creature is compelled to pursue the directive for the remainder of the Scene. At the start of each affected creature's turn, it may attempt to resist again with a POW roll; on a success, the compulsion ends for that creature. The creature retains control of its Actions and may act intelligently, but will not willingly act against the directive while affected.
career_mindbender_planted_certainty_rule: Type: Action (Imprint Belief) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose one creature within Short range and roll Magic (Enchantment) opposed by the target's POW. On a success, you implant a single plausible belief about the current situation. Until the end of the Scene, the target treats this belief as true when making decisions, rationalizing contradictory information rather than rejecting it outright. This belief does not override direct sensory evidence, compel specific actions, or alter identity or loyalties.
career_mindbender_planted_impulse_rules-: Type: Reaction (Reposition) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: A creature within Short range takes an action or movement on its turn. Effect: Roll Magic (Enchantment) opposed by the target's POW. On a success, the target immediately moves toward or away from a creature of your choice — up to its normal movement. This occurs before the triggering action resolves. The target cannot choose to resist the movement. This cannot force movement that would be directly self-destructive, such as off a ledge or into hazardous terrain.
career_mindbender_press_the_advantage_ru: Type: Free Action (Momentum) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: After you succeed on a social skill or Magic (Enchantment) roll against a target. Your next opposed roll this Scene directly targeting that same creature gains +1 bonus die.
career_mindbender_psychic_aegis_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Counter) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You or an ally within Short range is targeted by an Enchantment spell, mind-affecting ability, or fear effect. Effect: The triggering effect automatically fails. The source of the effect must roll POW opposed by your Magic (Enchantment) or become Shaken until the end of its next turn as the psychic energy rebounds.
career_mindbender_psychic_lash_rules-u: Type: Action (Strike) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Target one creature within Short range. Roll Magic (Enchantment) opposed by the target's POW. On a success, deal 1d6 Veil damage.
career_mindbender_psychic_scream_rules-u: Type: Action (Burst) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — All hostile creatures within Short range must roll POW opposed by your Magic (Enchantment). On a failure, the creature takes 1d6 Veil damage and becomes Shaken until the end of its next turn. On a success, the creature is unaffected.
career_mindbender_psychic_shell_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Protect) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You are targeted by an Enchantment spell, fear effect, Intimidation, or any ability that targets POW or mental resistance. Effect: You gain +1 bonus die to resist the triggering effect.
career_mindbender_puppet_string_rules-u: Type: Action (Command) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Target one creature within Short range. Roll Magic (Enchantment) opposed by the target's POW. On a success, you choose one Action the target takes on its next turn. You select the Action type and its target, but the creature executes it using its own skills and abilities. You cannot force self-destructive actions, once-per-session abilities, or consumable use. The target retains its movement and Reactions. After the effect ends, the target is aware its behavior was influenced.
career_mindbender_read_the_room_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Assess) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After observing a target's behavior or exchanging words with them, roll Insight opposed by the target's POW. On a success, the GM answers one question truthfully about the target's current emotional driver, confidence level, or motivation, and whether they are inclined to escalate, withdraw, or deflect under pressure this scene.
career_mindbender_strain_buffer_rules-u: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum Strain by +1. This talent may appear in multiple Career Trees.
career_mindbender_subtle_suggestion_rule: Type: Free Action (Setup) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Before casting an Enchantment spell that influences thoughts or emotions, gain +1 bonus die on the spellcasting roll.
career_mindbender_thought_leech_rules-u: Type: Action (Extract) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Target one creature within Short range. Roll Magic (Enchantment) opposed by the target's POW. On a success, the GM reveals one of the following: the target's intended action next turn, its primary target, or one vulnerability or fear. You also gain +1 bonus die on your next roll directly opposed by that target this Scene.
career_mindbender_turn_the_knife_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Exploit) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When a creature within Short range suffers a condition or limitation caused by one of your Mindbender talents, choose one allied creature within Short range of you. That ally may immediately take one Maneuver that does not provoke Reactions.
career_mindbender_unassailable_mind_rule: Type: Reaction • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You are targeted by a social effect, Enchantment spell, interrogation, Intimidation, or any attempt to influence, read, or destabilize your mental or emotional state. Effect: The triggering effect automatically fails. Until the end of the Scene, hostile creatures cannot gain actionable insight into your thoughts, emotions, intentions, or leverage through social skills or mind-affecting effects. Attempts may still occur, but they provide no mechanical or narrative advantage.
career_operator_close_quarters_ready_rul: Type: Free Action (CQB) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — This attack only: reduce the close-quarters penalty by one step when firing a Handgun or SMG at Engaged range.
career_operator_combat_presence_rules-u: Type: Passive (Discipline Aura) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — While you are not Suppressed and not behind Full Cover, allies within Short range gain +10% to Dodge and POW rolls made to resist Suppression effects.
career_operator_combat_stride_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Reposition) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After you hit with a ranged attack, take one Move Maneuver. This movement follows all normal rules, including triggering Reactions. It may move you into Cover or briefly break Line of Sight, but it cannot remove you from the current combat or move you more than one range band.
career_operator_controlled_pair_rules-u: Type: Attack modifier • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When firing in Semi-Auto, reduce the penalty die on your second shot by one step. This does not affect the first or third shot and does not stack with other effects that reduce Semi-Auto penalties.
career_operator_cover_discipline_rules-u: Type: Passive (Cover) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you Dodge while in Cover, gain +1 bonus die on that Dodge.
career_operator_deadzone_entry_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (CQB) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you move into Engaged range, ignore CQB penalties for your next Handgun or SMG attack. The attack must be taken immediately after entering Engaged.
career_operator_endurance_conditioning_r: Type: Passive (Resilience) • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum HP by +2.
career_operator_fire_and_fade_rules-u: Type: Passive (Stride Upgrade) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you move at least one range band during your turn after making a ranged attack, you may immediately enter Cover at the end of that movement. If this movement occurs after a successful hit, it does not trigger Reactions, even if it would normally do so.
career_operator_grim_resolve_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Ignore) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After you roll a Malfunction result or become Suppressed, you may ignore that effect entirely.
career_operator_hard_coded_reflexes_rule: Type: Reaction (Clear Jam) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: Your firearm malfunctions. Effect: Attempt to clear the malfunction as a Free Action instead of an Action (requires a successful firearm skill roll to clear).
career_operator_hard_exit_protocol_rules: Type: Reaction (Extraction) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: A hostile scene turns against the crew (an ally is incapacitated, the team becomes pinned, or an escape route is about to be cut off). You immediately designate yourself and up to three allies within Medium range. Each designated character may take one Move Maneuver as a Free Action. This movement does not trigger Reactions, Suppression shots, or attacks of opportunity, and may be used to break Line of Sight, reach Cover, or disengage from enemies. These Maneuvers must be resolved immediately or the opportunity is lost.
career_operator_kinetic_retaliation_rule: Type: Reaction (Return Fire) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You are hit by a ranged attack while in Partial Cover. You may immediately make a Handgun or SMG attack against the attacker before damage is applied; if the attack hits, reduce the damage you take by 2 (minimum 1).
career_operator_last_man_doctrine_rules-: Type: Reaction (Refusal) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you would be incapacitated or reduced to 0 HP, you remain active until the end of your next turn. During this window, you ignore wound penalties, Suppression, and Malfunction effects. You may act normally, but you cannot regain HP during this duration.
career_operator_precision_reacquired_rul: Type: Special immediate (Reroll) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After a failed ranged attack, reroll it. On a success, add +1 damage.
career_operator_quick_reload_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Reload) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Reload a firearm as a Free Action instead of a Maneuver.
career_operator_quickdraw_rules-u: Type: Action (Draw & Fire) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Draw and fire a Handgun or SMG as a single Action. Cannot be used if Surprised.
career_operator_rapid_clearance_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (Evade) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After a successful ranged attack against a target you have not attacked earlier this round and while not Engaged, make a Dodge. On a success, you may move one range band as a Free Action.
career_operator_saturation_fire_rules-u: Type: Attack modifier • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When firing Burst Fire or Full Auto, ignore one penalty die on the attack. This does not stack with other effects that reduce penalty dice.
career_operator_shatterpoint_protocol_ru: Type: Special immediate (Damage Optimization) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After you hit with a Semi-Auto or Burst Fire attack, roll damage twice and choose which result to apply. That attack also gains Armor Piercing 2.
career_operator_strain_buffer_rules-u: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum Strain by +1. (This talent may appear in multiple Career Trees.)
career_operator_suppression_discipline_r: Type: Free Action (Suppress) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you miss with Full Auto, treat the attack as a hit for Suppression only (no damage).
career_operator_sweep_and_reposition_rul: Type: Special immediate (Chain) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After you down a target with a ranged attack, immediately move one range band and make one Burst Fire or Single Shot attack against a new target.
career_operator_tactical_apex_rules-u: Type: Attack modifier • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When attacking with a Burst Fire weapon attack, reduce the number of penalty dice by 1 (to a minimum of 0) for that attack. On a hit, deal +1 damage.
career_operator_tactical_lock_in_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Designation) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After you hit a target with a ranged attack, designate that enemy. For the next two rounds, your first attack each round against that target gains +1 bonus die, and that target suffers 1 penalty die on attacks made against you.
career_operator_zeroed_vector_killbox_ru: Type: Action (Mark) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose one visible enemy within Medium range. Until the end of the round, you and all allies gain +1 bonus die on ranged attacks against that target. If you are the first to hit the target during this window, your attack deals +2 damage.
career_pathfinder_apex_predator_rules-u: Type: Action (Perfect Strike) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose one visible creature you have personally observed or identified this Scene through Track, Veil Lore, or Veil-Sign. Make one attack against that creature. This attack ignores all Armor. On a hit, deal maximum damage for your weapon and the target is Stunned until the end of their next turn. Creatures immune to the Stunned condition instead become Impaired until the end of their next turn. This talent cannot be used against a creature you have not personally observed or identified this Scene.
career_pathfinder_be_havioral_read_rules: Type: Reaction (Prediction) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: Your designated Quarry declares a movement or attack. Effect: Before the action resolves, impose 1 penalty die on that action. This talent requires an active Quarry designation to function and represents deliberate pattern recognition rather than supernatural ability.
career_pathfinder_blood_and_dust_rules-u: Type: Passive (Tracking Enhancement) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you succeed on a Track roll, you automatically extract additional detail based on your success tier. Regular Success: number of targets, direction of travel, and approximate time since passage. Hard Success: physical condition of the quarry (wounded, burdened, exhausted) and pace of movement. Extreme or Critical Success: behavioral state of the quarry (fleeing, hunting, disoriented) and whether they are aware they are being followed. This information is provided in addition to any other successful Track results and does not require an additional roll.
career_pathfinder_breaking_point_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Exploitation Strike) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You successfully hit a Veil entity or creature you have identified this Scene through Veil Lore or Track. Effect: That attack deals maximum possible damage for all damage dice rolled, applied before armor and other modifiers. This effect applies to one attack only and cannot be combined with other effects that also maximize damage dice. Strain is rolled and spent at the moment of declaration, before the damage roll.
career_pathfinder_corner_of_the_eye_rule: Type: Free Action (Activate) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare at the start of your turn or before making a Perception or Listen roll. Until the end of the Scene, you are in a heightened state of peripheral awareness. If any hidden or concealed creature moves within Medium range of you during this window, the GM will notify you and you may immediately make a Perception roll opposed by that creature's Stealth to pinpoint their exact location. On a success, you may share their position with any ally within Short range as a Free Action. This talent must be active before the triggering movement occurs — it cannot be declared retroactively.
career_pathfinder_cut_off_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Pursuit Interrupt) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: Your designated Quarry attempts to move away from you, Disengage, or reposition to a more distant range band. Effect: Before the Quarry's movement resolves, you may immediately move one range band toward them as a Free Action. If this movement brings you within Engaged or Short range, your Quarry suffers 1 penalty die on the triggering movement or action. This movement follows all normal rules and may trigger Reactions against you. This talent requires an active Quarry designation and provides no benefit if your Quarry is not designated.
career_pathfinder_dead_ground_rules-u: Type: Passive (Terrain Movement) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Moving through natural difficult terrain — undergrowth, unstable rubble, steep slopes, deep water, ley-burned ground — costs you one fewer Maneuver than normal, to a minimum of one. This talent does not apply to terrain made difficult by spells, constructed barriers, or technology.
career_pathfinder_eyes_on_the_quarry_rul: Type: Free Action (Mark) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — After succeeding on a Track, Perception, or Veil-Sign roll against a specific creature this Scene, you may designate that creature as your Quarry. While a Quarry is designated, you gain +1 bonus die on the first attack you make against them each round. Constructs, drones, and spirits cannot be designated as a Quarry — this talent requires a living, corporeal target whether creature or sentient being, whose behavioral patterns and physical passage can be read. Only one Quarry may be active at a time. The designation ends when your Quarry is incapacitated, killed, or leaves the Scene permanently. See Quarry Designation Rule.
career_pathfinder_ghost_country_rules-u: Type: Action (Vanish the Trail) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose up to five allies within Short range. Until you declare this effect ended or the Scene concludes, your entire group moves through all terrain — including arcane hazard zones and Veil-warped ground — as normal terrain with no additional Maneuver cost. This removes terrain-based movement penalties only and does not protect against Veil radiation, environmental damage, or hazard exposure in those zones. Your group leaves no trackable evidence of passage: no physical traces, thermal signatures, mundane sensor readings, or arcane residue unless deliberately created. Any creature attempting to Track your group this Scene must do so at Extreme difficulty. Allies must remain within Short range of you to maintain these benefits.
career_pathfinder_groundwork_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Stealth Recovery) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — At the end of your turn, if you are in natural terrain, heavy cover, ruins, or an area without strong artificial lighting, you may immediately attempt to enter Stealth as a Free Action. This attempt follows all normal Stealth rules but does not consume your Action or Maneuver.
career_pathfinder_hollow_watch_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Observation) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare before making a Perception or Listen roll. You have not voluntarily moved this round. Gain +1 bonus die on that roll.
career_pathfinder_hostile_ground_rules-u: Type: Passive (Environmental Resilience) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you make a roll to resist or endure an environmental hazard — toxic atmosphere, extreme temperature, choking smoke, corrosive precipitation, unstable terrain, or a comparable scene-level condition — you gain +1 bonus die on that roll. This talent does not remove damage already dealt by a hazard, does not extend its benefit to allies, and does not prevent or affect the effects of Veil radiation.
career_pathfinder_hunters_knowledge_rule: Type: Passive (Entity Exploitation) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — After successfully identifying a Veil entity through Veil-Sign or a Veil Lore roll this Scene, your attacks against that entity gain Armor Piercing 1. If your identification roll was a Hard Success or better, your attacks against that entity also deal +1 damage until the end of the Scene. These bonuses apply only to the specific entity identified and do not extend to other entities of the same type encountered later in the Scene.
career_pathfinder_iron_trail_rules-u: Type: Action (Lead Element) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose up to three allies within Short range. Until the end of the Scene, those allies benefit from your Dead Ground talent while moving as a group and benefit from Trail Hardened's environmental Strain cost reduction. Allies must remain within Short range of you to maintain these benefits. An ally who separates loses the benefits until they return to Short range on a subsequent round. This talent does not grant allies any other Pathfinder talents.
career_pathfinder_no_safe_ground_rules-u: Type: Action (Kill Zone) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Establish a Short-radius kill zone centered on a point within Medium range that you can see. Until the end of the Scene, all hostile creatures within that zone suffer 1 penalty die on all actions, cannot benefit from Cover within it, and must spend one additional Maneuver to exit the zone voluntarily. At the start of each hostile creature's turn while inside the zone, they must succeed on a POW roll or become Suppressed. You and allies you designate at the time of activation are unaffected. If you move beyond Medium range from the zone's center point, the effect ends immediately.
career_pathfinder_pinned_ground_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Zone Control) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose one visible creature within Medium range. Until the start of your next turn, that creature's voluntary movement is limited: moving beyond Short range from their current position costs one additional Maneuver. If the creature attempts forced movement to bypass this restriction, roll Track or Perception opposed by their Athletics — on a success, the forced movement is reduced by one range band. This effect represents active positional pressure and read, not physical restraint. It ends immediately if you move beyond Medium range from the target or are incapacitated.
career_pathfinder_read_the_wind_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Field Awareness) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you would be Surprised by a creature or person, you may immediately make a Perception roll before the Surprised condition is applied. On any success, the Surprised condition is negated. This talent applies in natural terrain, ruins, collapsed districts, fringe zones, and other environments where concealment is shaped primarily by terrain rather than active urban surveillance or formal interior security. This talent does not apply to supernatural sources of surprise — spirits, Veil entities, and arcane concealment effects may still Surprise you normally.
career_pathfinder_shadow_of_the_fringe_r: Type: Free Action (Total Concealment) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Immediately enter a state of complete environmental concealment. Until you attack or the Scene ends, you cannot be targeted by ranged attacks, spells, or abilities that require confirmed line of sight. Creatures that have previously pinpointed your position may still target the space you occupy, but must first succeed on a Perception roll opposed by your Stealth with 2 penalty dice. While this state is active, you may take one Move Maneuver each round as a Free Action. This state ends immediately if you attack, take a loud action, or choose to reveal yourself. You must be in natural terrain, significant cover, ruins, or low-light conditions to activate this talent.
career_pathfinder_silent_country_rules-u: Type: Passive (Movement Concealment) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Moving through natural difficult terrain or heavy cover does not automatically reveal your position or break your Stealth. You must still succeed on Stealth rolls when challenged, but the act of moving through these environments alone does not trigger detection checks against you.
career_pathfinder_spotters_call_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Target Designation) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose one visible creature you have located this Scene through Track, Perception, Corner of the Eye, or Veil-Sign. Until the start of your next turn, all allies within Medium range gain +10% on their first ranged or melee attack against that target. If that target attempts to move to cover or disengage before the start of your next turn, they must spend one additional Maneuver to do so. This +10% bonus and movement cost increase do not stack with similar talent-granted percentage bonuses or Maneuver costs applied to the same target simultaneously.
career_pathfinder_strain_buffer_rules-u: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum Strain by +1. (This talent may appear in multiple Career Trees.)
career_pathfinder_terrain_ghost_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Environmental Concealment) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Enter a concealment state that persists until you attack, cast a spell, take a loud action, or move more than one range band in a single turn. While in this state, you cannot be targeted by attacks or abilities that require confirmed line of sight from creatures that have not already pinpointed your position this round. Creatures without a confirmed fix on you suffer 1 penalty die on any detection attempt against you. Creatures may still target your space blindly or with area effects as normal if they have reason to suspect your position. You must be in natural terrain, heavy cover, ruins, or low-light conditions to activate this talent.
career_pathfinder_the_hunt_ends_here_rul: Type: Action (Apex Pursuit) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Declare one visible creature within Medium range as your Prey. Your Quarry designation is immediately applied to this target without requiring a Track roll. Until the end of the Scene, once per round you may take one Move Maneuver toward your Prey as a Free Action that does not trigger Reactions. Each time your Prey attempts to move away from you, leave the Scene, or increase their distance from you by more than one range band in a single round, they must succeed on an Athletics roll opposed by your Track or become Hobbled until the start of their next turn. When your Prey is reduced to 0 HP or captured, you immediately recover 2 Strain.
career_pathfinder_trail_hardened_rules-u: Type: Passive (Resilience) • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum HP by +2. Environmental Strain costs — from weather, toxic atmosphere, extreme temperature, and exposure hazards — are reduced by 1, to a minimum of 0.
career_pathfinder_veil_sign_rules-u: Type: Passive (Entity Reading) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you first observe a Veil entity in a Scene, make a Veil Lore roll without spending an Action. Regular Success: identify entity type, behavioral state (hunting, territorial, feeding, retreating), and obvious physical vulnerabilities. Hard Success: additionally determine whether the entity is acting autonomously or under external direction. Extreme or Critical Success: additionally reveal one specific condition, damage type, or tactical circumstance that would force the entity to disengage or trigger a behavioral response. On a failure, no information is gained.
career_pilot_aerial_pivot_shot_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Shot on the Move) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — While piloting or drone-rigged, fire one handheld ranged weapon as a Free Action during vehicle movement or repositioning, ignoring penalty dice caused by unstable footing or active motion.
career_pilot_airspace_denial_vector_rule: Type: Free Action (Zone Control) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After you win Initiative while piloting or drone-rigged, designate an aerial control zone within Short of your vehicle or drone. Until the start of your next turn, enemies that enter, move within, or act in this zone suffer 1 penalty die on all actions.
career_pilot_black_sky_suppression_rules: Type: Action (Electronic Warfare) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — For one round, all enemy drones, vehicles, and remote systems within Medium suffer 1 penalty die on all actions and cannot benefit from bonus dice. This effect disrupts coordination and targeting but does not disable systems.
career_pilot_burn_vector_gambit_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (Reposition + Act) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After a drone or vehicle you control completes its turn, reposition it anywhere within Short, then immediately make one attack or system action with it. This movement ignores overwatch, elevation, and terrain penalties.
career_pilot_combat_integration_mesh_rul: Type: Special immediate (Order Relay) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Once per round, when you hit a target while piloting or drone-rigged, you may immediately command one drone you control to take one simple action (move, basic attack, or utility) as a Free Action. This does not require or consume a command action and does not stack with other effects that grant additional drone actions.
career_pilot_combat_rigging_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (Deploy) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you deploy a combat-capable drone, it may immediately take one simple action (move or aim) as part of the deployment.
career_pilot_deadzone_reflex_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (Pre-turn Move) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — In a vehicle combat or pursuit scene, immediately after Initiative is rolled, move your vehicle 1 range band as a Free Maneuver before any character takes a turn.
career_pilot_drone_lock_protocol_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Lock-On) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After a drone you control hits a target, designate that target. Until the end of your next turn, that drone gains +1 bonus die on attacks against the designated target. You may choose to extend this effect into subsequent turns by spending 2 Strain at the start of each of your turns. This effect ends immediately if the drone switches targets.
career_pilot_drop_vector_override_rules-: Type: Action (Approach) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — During a rapid or evasive landing approach, all actions targeting your aircraft, drone, or anyone onboard suffer 1 penalty die until the start of your next turn. You suffer no Piloting or vehicle control penalties during this descent.
career_pilot_endurance_conditioning_rule: Type: Passive (Resilience) • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum HP by +2.
career_pilot_jet_wash_drift_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (Debuff) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — After you succeed on a Piloting evade or maneuver, choose one nearby vehicle, drone, or enemy within Short; that target suffers 1 penalty die on its next Gunnery or Ranged Weapon attack.
career_pilot_overclock_sync_node_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Reroll) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: A drone or vehicle you control fails a roll. Effect: Immediately after resolving the failure, reroll that roll with +1 bonus die.
