Excels at charm, coercion, fear, pacification, and emotional manipulation—whether subtle or overwhelming. Governs spells that alter behavior or perception. Casting typically targets another creature and uses contested rolls; investigative or social uses may be rolled directly.
magic_illusion_desc-u: The shaping of perception through arcane means—bending sight, sound, smell, or other senses to deceive the mind. Illusionists craft phantasms, veil themselves in shadow, or conjure impossible sights. Governs the casting and interpretation of illusion-based spells. Relies on subtlety and creativity more than brute power.
magic_necromancy_desc-u: The arcane manipulation of death, decay, and the soul. Reaches into the boundary between life and death to raise the dead, drain vitality, bind spirits, or speak with the deceased. Often feared or outlawed, necromantic practices are powerful and deeply entwined with moral and spiritual risk. Most combat spells use normal casting rolls; ritual uses may require direct skill checks.
magic_restoration_desc-u: The healing and purifying branch of arcana, used to mend wounds, neutralize poisons, remove curses, and restore physical or spiritual vitality. Channels stabilizing energies to knit flesh, soothe trauma, or realign disrupted life force. Essential for battlefield triage, long-term recovery, and spiritual cleansing.
magic_summoning_desc-u: The arcane discipline of calling, negotiating with, and controlling entities from beyond the material realm—spirits, eidolons, echoes of the dead, elemental projections, and other Veil-crossing beings. Governs the circles, invocations, Contracts, and bindings required to call and control summoned entities. The act of summoning often creates ripples that attract unpredictable attention from the Veiled Realms.
magic_technomancy_desc-u: A hybrid arcane discipline focused on the fusion of magic and advanced technology. Enables interaction with archanotech systems, digital constructs, and magical networks embedded within machines. Practitioners manipulate data streams, awaken semi-sentient spellware, and disrupt or empower magi-tech devices through arcane code.
magic_universal_desc-u: Represents broad or cross-school magical aptitude not bound to a single tradition or element. Used when engaging with hybrid spells, unknown magical phenomena, or arcane effects that resist easy classification. Allows identification and analysis of diverse magical sources, though specialized skills remain more effective within their own domains.
magic_warding_desc-u: Protective arcana used to shield the caster or others from harm—physical, magical, or spiritual. Includes barriers, nullification fields, resistance glyphs, and protective counterspells. Governs the casting and recognition of protective enchantments, magical countermeasures, and anti-magic zones.
mark_of_the_faithful_desc-u: A visible or concealed symbol, scar, implant, or token that identifies you to those who know what to look for.
mechanics_desc-u: Covers physical systems—engines, structural components, powered armor joints, hydraulics, and moving parts. Used to maintain, repair, and modify machines ranging from elevators and vehicles to prosthetic limbs and industrial exosuits. While Electronics governs circuitry, Mechanics handles the physical systems those circuits control.
medicine_desc-u: Covers advanced medical knowledge and techniques, including diagnosis, surgical procedures, treatment of disease, long-term care, and trauma response. Unlike First Aid, which handles immediate stabilization, Medicine is used for sustained treatment, complex operations, and managing conditions over time—especially those involving arcane, cybernetic, or mutated biology.
melee_weapons_desc-u: Covers the use of handheld close-combat weapons such as swords, knives, clubs, batons, axes, energy blades, and arcane-forged weapons. Determines both offensive and defensive proficiency in melee engagements, whether through formal martial training or brutal street-fighting experience. As a Combat Skill, Melee Weapons cannot be Pushed.
middle_manager_desc-u: Knows procedures, bottlenecks, and who to bribe to move things quietly.
mod_bio_coded_effect-u: Weapon operates only for its registered user. Removal costs half base weapon price and a Hard Mechanics roll — on a failure, the weapon is destroyed.
mod_compensator_effect-u: SA mode only. Second shot: no penalty die. Third shot: one penalty die instead of two. Cannot use with BF or FA.
mod_internal_recoil_system_effect-u: Reduces the penalty die cost of BF and FA by one. Cannot reduce below zero.
mod_magnification_scope_effect-u: Rifles only. One penalty die at Short range or closer. One bonus die at Long and Extreme range.
mod_precision_choke_effect-u: Shotguns only. Converts scatter attack into a single-target strike — no cone, no Scatter Spray.
mod_quick_load_system_effect-u: Magazine and clip-fed weapons only. Once per scene, reload as a Free Action instead of a Maneuver.
mod_quick_select_trigger_module_effect-u: Fire mode switching becomes a Free Action. Does not require Smartlink cyberware.
mod_reflex_sight_effect-u: +10% to hit when using Aim at Short or Medium range. Does not stack with Smartlink bonus.
mod_silencer_effect-u: Pistols only. Removes Loud trait. SS mode only. Shots unnoticed unless within Short range. +10% to Stealth when firing while hidden.
mod_slug_barrel_effect-u: Shotguns only. Treat maximum range as one band further for range penalty calculations.
mod_smartlink_effect-u: +10% to hit. One bonus die on Called Shots. Mode switching becomes a Free Action. Requires Smartlink cyberware.
mod_suppressor_effect-u: SMGs, ARs, Snipers. Removes Loud trait. SA mode only. Stealth roll with one penalty die to avoid detection after firing.
mod_thermal_scope_effect-u: Rifles and SMGs only. Ignores attack roll penalties from low-light conditions or smoke at Medium range or closer.
natural_world_desc-u: Covers knowledge of ecosystems, plant life, animal behavior, geological formations, weather patterns, and natural hazards. Used to identify flora and fauna (including post-Cataclysm mutations), track migration patterns, read signs of environmental instability, or understand how terrain has been shaped by time, magic, or disaster. Often used alongside Survival, but focuses on comprehension rather than application.
navigate_desc-u: Measures a character's ability to find their way through unfamiliar environments—whether winding through the ruins of Old Arcadia, charting a safe course through arcane stormfronts, or moving unseen across sector boundaries. Includes map reading, route plotting, astral positioning, dead reckoning, and interpreting magical waymarkers or forgotten infrastructure grids.
neighborhood_fixer_desc-u: Knows who owes favors, who can be leaned on, and who to avoid altogether.
occult_lore_desc-u: Represents scholarly or experiential knowledge of magical traditions, supernatural beliefs, rituals, folklore, and mystical phenomena from across the post-Cataclysm world. Does not grant the ability to cast spells but allows recognition of cults, symbols, taboos, and the surface-level mechanics of magic practiced outside formal training.
old_crew_contact_desc-u: A former associate who knows your history and what lines you won’t cross.
otherskill3_desc-u: A custom skill slot for a player-defined ability not covered by existing skill categories. Name and description must be agreed upon with the GM before use. Follows standard skill mechanics unless the GM specifies otherwise.
perception_desc-u: Governs a character's ability to notice details, threats, hidden objects, or suspicious behaviors in their environment. Includes spotting concealed doors, ambushers, planted items, surveillance devices, or arcane anomalies. A critical tool for investigation, infiltration, and counter-surveillance. Cybernetic augmentations or spell-enhanced senses may modify this skill, but even the best optics can be fooled without training and focus.
perform_acting_desc-u: The ability to embody a character convincingly in theatrical, social, or undercover contexts. Covers scripted performance and improvised roleplay alike. Used to entertain, distract, or sustain a false persona in front of an audience—whether on a stage, in a boardroom, or mid-infiltration.
perform_dance_desc-u: Covers movement-based performance art—from formal dance traditions to ritual movement and improvised street expression. Used to captivate audiences, participate in cultural ceremonies, or perform in competitive or arcane contexts where physical expressiveness matters.
perform_impersonation_desc-u: The ability to convincingly mimic another person's voice, posture, behavioral patterns, emotional cadence, and social habits. Not just voice acting—it is identity mimicry, used to fool gatekeepers, infiltrate social spaces, or impersonate someone for performance or deception. Requires prior personal observation of the target.
perform_instrument_desc-u: Covers technical proficiency with a specific musical instrument. Must be specified at time of purchase (e.g., guitar, synthesizer, arcane resonance harp). Used to perform in live settings, compose pieces, or signal through music in covert operations. Related instruments may provide minor synergy but are not interchangeable.
perform_ritual_desc-u: Covers performance within formal magical or cultural ceremonies—including rites of passage, arcane invocations, spiritual observances, and structured House or clan rituals. A flawless ritual performance can strengthen magical outcomes, earn social standing, or appease powerful entities.
perform_vocal_desc-u: Covers singing, chanting, and vocal performance—whether in traditional song, arcane resonance chanting, or emotionally charged spoken word. A powerful vocalist can sway moods, carry encoded messages, or serve as a focal point in ritual and performance alike.
performance_archive_desc-u: A private file of reports, evaluations, and internal memos that can be referenced to establish credibility—or leverage.
perk_blood_born_survivor_description-u: Once per campaign, when you would be killed or rendered permanently incapacitated, you survive. You immediately fall unconscious and are removed from the scene. You suffer severe narrative consequences determined by the GM. This Perk does not prevent capture, trauma, or lasting harm, it only ensures survival.
perk_combat_veteran_description-u: Once per session, you may declare a Dodge or Fight Back without paying its Strain cost. All other rules and consequences apply normally.
perk_combat_veteran_flavor-u: You've survived worse. You don’t flinch—you move.
perk_corp_groomed_description-u: Once per session, when dealing with corporate, House, or institutional actors, you may invoke your background to gain limited access, secure an introduction, or delay consequences long enough to act. This does not grant authority, resources, or immunity — only a temporary opening.
perk_corp_groomed_flavor-u: You know the language of power—and how to speak it.
perk_danger_sense_description-u: Once per session, when an ambush, trap, or sudden threat is about to resolve, you may immediately reposition, take cover, or adjust your stance before initiative is rolled. This does not negate the danger, it changes how you meet it.
perk_danger_sense_flavor-u: You feel tension before it breaks.
perk_dirty_fighter_description-u: Once per scene, when making a melee attack against a target already engaged with another character, you deal an additional +1d4 damage.
perk_dirty_fighter_flavor-u: You strike when the other guy isn’t looking.
perk_final_stand_description-u: Once per session, when you are reduced to 0 HP, you may immediately take a single final action before collapsing unconscious. This action is taken with one bonus die. The action cannot restore HP, negate the damage taken, or prevent unconsciousness.
perk_forgettable_presence_description-u: Once per session, when an NPC, witness, or system successfully identifies, recognizes, or recalls you, you may downgrade that result. Descriptions become vague, footage loses clarity, or pursuit details become unreliable. This does not erase records already secured.
perk_forgettable_presence_flavor-u: You vanish into crowds and conversations like smoke.
perk_hard_preparation_description-u: Once per session, before a dangerous situation resolves, you may declare that you prepared for this moment. Introduce one plausible piece of equipment, positioning advantage, or contingency that your character could reasonably have arranged earlier. The GM may impose a cost for this preparation, such as Strain, lost time, exposure, or narrative fallout tied to how it was acquired. This Perk cannot create rare or restricted gear, bypass established scarcity, or contradict previously established fiction.
perk_institutional_weight_description-u: Choose one corporation, Magi House, syndicate, or recognized institution at character creation. Once per session, when dealing with that institution or its agents, you may invoke your standing to force the situation to slow down. An NPC or system must acknowledge you as someone who matters: procedures escalate, authority is consulted, or consequences are delayed long enough for you to act. This does not grant obedience, resources, or immunity. The institution will remember the disruption, and the GM may introduce scrutiny, obligation, or fallout later. Additional institutions may only be added through Reputation, narrative advancement, or explicit GM approval.
perk_last_clear_thought_description-u: Once per session, when you fail a roll that would cause loss of control — such as panic, possession, domination, forced surrender, or compelled flight — you may immediately reroll that check and take the new result. This Perk cannot be used on pushed rolls, Luck rolls, or purely physical damage rolls.
perk_linguist_description-u: Choose one additional language. You are natively fluent and literate in both your primary and secondary languages, including slang, dialects, and professional jargon. You suffer no penalties for operating in multilingual or coded environments.
perk_linguist_flavor-u: You speak like you were born in two places.
perk_mind_leash_description-u: Once per session, when you would be affected by fear, possession, domination, mental control, or arcane suppression, you may automatically resist the effect. You must be conscious and aware of the attempt.
perk_mind_leash_flavor-u: Your will doesn’t just hold—it locks shut.
perk_photographic_recall_description-u: Once per session, you may declare that your character perfectly recalls a specific detail they have personally seen, heard, or read before. This recall requires no roll and provides clear, factual information appropriate to the original exposure.
perk_photographic_recall_flavor-u: You never forget what you see.
perk_resilient_frame_description-u: Once per scene, when you take physical damage, reduce that damage by a small fixed amount determined by the GM based on scale and severity. This reduction cannot negate catastrophic damage or environmental hazards.
perk_scorched_soul_description-u: Once per session, when you would be affected by supernatural fear, Veil-induced dread, hallucination, or mental stress, you may ignore the effects entirely. You still perceive the phenomenon — you simply refuse to break.
perk_scorched_soul_flavor-u: You've endured something that would break most minds.
perk_shadow_mark_description-u: Spirits and Veil entities instinctively recognize you. Once per session, when interacting with a spirit or navigating a Veil-influenced zone, you gain one bonus die on a single relevant roll. Veil-sensitive entities may also single you out or react with fear or hostility.
perk_shadow_mark_flavor-u: Something from the other side remembers you.
perk_tactical_instinct_description-u: Once per session, at the start of a combat scene, you may declare Tactical Instinct. You act first in initiative order during the first round of combat, regardless of rolls. After your action resolves, normal initiative resumes.
perk_tactical_instinct_flavor-u: You see the opening before anyone else even moves.
perk_veil_anchor_description-u: Once per session, when a Veil effect, anomaly, or supernatural phenomenon would displace, disorient, or forcibly move you, you remain grounded. You still suffer any associated damage or consequences.
perk_veil_drenched_intuition_description: Once per session, you may still yourself and receive a symbolic impression from the GM about the current situation — a sensation, image, or omen that hints at danger or opportunity without confirming outcomes.
perk_veil_drenched_intuition_flavor-u: Something in you dreams the truth before it happens.
personal_sidearm_package_desc-u: A legally registered or discreetly unregistered sidearm with spare magazines and carry gear.
personal_stash_desc-u: A small hidden cache containing supplies, a weapon-sized space, or valuable items placed somewhere only you know how to reach.
persuade_desc-u: Reflects your ability to guide others toward a belief, course of action, or compromise using logic, empathy, or carefully framed arguments. Unlike Deception or Intimidate, Persuade relies on sincerity—or at least the appearance of it. Often used in negotiations, diplomacy, or convincing a reluctant ally to take a risk.
pharma_adrenamax_effect-u: +10% to all attack rolls and bonus die on Initiative for scene duration. Aftermath: 1d6 Strain from withdrawal.
pharma_bioknit_recovery_patch_effect-u: Bonus die on Major Wound recovery roll during downtime rest. No HP restored. No effect during active scenes.
pharma_calm_effect-u: Removes Panicked condition. +10% to POW rolls vs. Panicked for scene duration. −10 Initiative while active.
pharma_clarity_effect-u: +10% to Analyze, Research, and Interface for scene duration. Aftermath: 2d6 Strain from cognitive fatigue.
pharma_neurocleen_effect-u: Bonus die on CON rolls vs. Veil-induced conditions for 1 hour. If already affected, immediate save may suppress for one scene.
pharma_pulseglass_effect-u: Perceive active magical auras, arcane signatures, and Veil currents for ~1 hour. Aftermath: CON roll or gain 1 level permanent Veil Corruption.
pharma_red_reign_effect-u: Immune to Impaired, Exhausted, Stunned; conscious at 0 HP for scene. Aftermath: 2d10 HP + 2d6 Strain damage bypassing all armor.
pharma_snapcharge_effect-u: Bonus die on all Initiative; one additional Reaction without spending Strain for scene. Aftermath: 3d6 Strain.
pharma_spiraldust_effect-u: +20% to Analyze, Interface, Research; perceive flux signatures within 10 meters for scene. Aftermath: 2d6 Strain + POW roll or 1 temp Veil Corruption level.
pharma_synthdream_effect-u: Immune to panic and fear conditions for 1d4 hours. Penalty die on all Analyze and Awareness checks while active.
pharma_veilkiss_effect-u: Cast one known spell without spending Strain, OR recover 3d6 Strain. Aftermath: CON roll or gain 1 level permanent Veil Corruption.
pharma_veillotion_effect-u: Patch: preventative; bonus die on CON rolls vs. Veil Corruption. Injection: may reverse one minor Veil Corruption level if used within 12 hours of exposure.
physics_desc-u: The study of force, motion, energy, magnetics, dimensional distortion, and theoretical frameworks—especially those warped by magic after the Cataclysm. Allows for understanding arcane-energy reactions, resonance fields, kinetic impact calculations, and how rift spaces or gravitic distortions operate. Used by arcane engineers, weapon designers, spell physicists, and mages blending natural law with arcane chaos.
pilot_aircraft_desc-u: Covers the operation of airborne vehicles, including traditional aircraft, tilt-rotors, gliders, and VTOLs. Governs takeoffs, landings, evasive maneuvers, and maintaining control in dangerous or low-visibility conditions. VTOL-specific actions such as hovering, drop insertions, or urban extraction under fire also fall under this skill.
pilot_mech_walker_desc-u: Covers the operation of bipedal and multiped mechanical walkers, including military Sentinels, industrial engineering rigs, corporate security walkers, and repurposed construction platforms. Governs all aspects of walker locomotion: balance, step patterns, recoil compensation, and management of actuators and gyrostabilizers.
pilot_watercraft_desc-u: Covers the navigation and operation of watercraft including boats, skimmers, submersibles, and arcane-powered sea vessels. Governs speed, control, and spatial awareness on aquatic surfaces—whether maneuvering through canals, docking in stormy weather, or pursuing targets across open water.
press_credentials_desc-u: Legitimate, expired, or forged access credentials that still open doors in the right situations.
private_security_handler_desc-u: Works for a corporation or contractor that values experience over paperwork and understands deniable operations.
protective_gear_desc-u: Reinforced gloves, respirator, eye protection, and industrial clothing that justify limited protection in hazardous environments.
racial_alteri_fixed_tell_rules-u:
No Alteri transformation is flawless. Each Alteri possesses a Fixed Tell: a subtle, immutable detail that persists across all forms. This may be a recurring vocal resonance, an unnatural stillness of the eyes, a faint but consistent scent, or another small inconsistency that resists change.

Rules:
You must define one Fixed Tell at character creation. This detail persists across all forms and cannot be removed or concealed by any means. In situations where a character deliberately scrutinizes you, suspects deception, or possesses relevant knowledge, the GM may grant a bonus die on checks made to identify you beneath a disguise. This feature never causes automatic discovery, but it always provides a plausible avenue for exposure.
racial_alteri_physical_authenticity_rule:
Alteri transformations have mass, texture, breath, and physical presence consistent with what they appear to be. Their altered form exists fully in the physical world rather than as a projected or arcane overlay.

Rules:
Your transformed form is not an illusion and cannot be dismissed, dispelled, or negated by effects that suppress or invalidate illusion magic. This does not protect you from physical inspection, biometric analysis, or magic designed to analyze altered bodies rather than illusions. This feature establishes plausibility under scrutiny; it does not confer immunity.
racial_alteri_shifting_form_rules-u:
Alteri possess a mutable physiology that allows them to physically reshape their body and voice to resemble another humanoid of similar size and structure. This transformation is biological rather than illusory: flesh alters, bone shifts, and vocal structure reconfigures. To most observers, an Alteri does not appear disguised—they appear real.

Rules:
You may physically assume the appearance and voice of any broadly humanoid ancestry or individual of comparable size. This does not alter your statistics, equipment, cyberware, credentials, or social authority, and does not grant knowledge or access you do not possess. Shifting Form is governed by detailed rules covering timing, Strain costs, scrutiny, and identity management; see Appendix: Alteri Masks & Shifting.
racial_alteri_social_resonance_rules-u:
Alteri are trained to sense when their presence, behavior, or presentation does not align with the social rhythm of a space. Through subconscious attention to posture, timing, tone, and reaction, they recognize instability before it becomes overt conflict.

Rules:
When you enter a sustained social interaction where your current identity, behavior, or presentation would plausibly create suspicion or instability, the GM must warn you before consequences escalate. This warning provides no mechanical benefit, reveals no intentions or solutions, and does not prevent consequences if you continue. It exists to prevent accidental escalation, not deliberate risk.
racial_draevi_economical_motion_rules-u:
Draevi movement is instinctive and economical. Whether crossing loose scree, broken infrastructure, or unstable elevation, they adjust without hesitation. Their balance is not careful; it is unconscious.

Rules:
When movement through unstable or hazardous terrain would impose a penalty, cost extra time, or force a balance check, you may treat the terrain as manageable for the purpose of repositioning, climbing, or crossing. This does not grant increased speed, prevent falls caused by external force, or negate deliberate sabotage.

Once per scene, if you fail a movement-related check, you may immediately reposition to nearby safe footing instead of suffering a fall or similar escalation.
racial_draevi_environmental_intuition_ru:
Draevi do not observe the world from a distance. They feel it through posture, vibration, airflow, and pressure. A Draevi knows when something is wrong not because they see it, but because the environment stops behaving as it should.

Rules:
Once per session, when you are in a physical environment with coherent terrain, structure, or natural flow, you may ask the GM whether an unseen environmental shift is occurring that would reasonably be noticeable to someone attuned to the space. The GM must answer truthfully, but vaguely. This may include approaching movement, structural instability, sudden unnatural stillness, or changes in crowd or wildlife behavior. This trait does not identify causes, threats, or intentions, and it does not function against purely digital, abstract, or fully arcane phenomena.
racial_draevi_inured_to_hardship_rules-u:
Draevi bodies are shaped by travel, exposure, and lean seasons. Their endurance is not immunity, but persistence—the ability to keep functioning through hardship that would force others to stop.

Rules:
Once per session, when you would suffer fatigue, environmental strain, or a lingering condition caused by weather, terrain, illness, or deprivation, you may delay the onset of that effect until the end of the scene. This does not negate damage, negate magical effects, or remove consequences; it allows you to function briefly through hardship before it catches up to you.
racial_draevi_the_living_ledger_rules-u:
Draevi culture records identity through the body. Tattoos, scarification, and ritual markings are testimony: proof of survival, oath, and transformation. These marks are visible, distinctive, and culturally significant—and difficult to hide without denying who you are.

Rules:
You must define at least one visible marking that reflects a significant event or truth in your past. This marking cannot be fully concealed without deliberate effort and appropriate clothing or disguise. In social or cultural contexts where Draevi traditions are known, this mark may invite recognition, expectation, or judgment. The GM is encouraged to treat these markings as narrative leverage rather than a benefit, especially when anonymity, neutrality, or denial would otherwise apply. This feature never causes automatic failure or forces disclosure; it exists to create persistent narrative pressure rather than impose mechanical penalties.
racial_feran_pack_intuition_rules-u: Once per session, as a Reaction, you may grant an ally within Short range a bonus die on a Dodge check. This must be declared before the roll is made.
racial_feran_reactive_momentum_rules-u: Once per session, when combat begins or an ambush is revealed, you may immediately reposition within Short range before Initiative is resolved. This movement must be physically plausible and cannot pass through solid barriers or occupied space. This does not grant an attack or negate surprise effects already triggered.
racial_feran_spatial_grace_rules-u: When climbing, leaping, or traversing vertical or unstable terrain, you may treat that terrain as manageable for the purpose of movement and positioning. Once per scene, if you would suffer damage from a fall you initiated or could reasonably anticipate, you may reduce that damage by half. This does not apply to forced movement or deliberate sabotage.
racial_feran_the_drive_to_pursue_rules-u: When a vulnerable target attempts to flee or retreat from you under pressure, the GM may present an instinctive pull to pursue. If you follow this impulse, you gain narrative momentum or advantageous positioning, but you must accept a complication (such as being separated from your allies or being lured into a trap). You may choose to resist this impulse with no penalty.
racial_human_a_reputation_for_friction_r:
Humans develop reputations by acting first and dealing with consequences later. Their choices leave impressions, stories, and loose ends that travel faster than they do. Even when their actions succeed, they are rarely forgotten.

Rules:
When you force momentum through early action, reckless commitment, or improvisation under pressure, the outcome tends to linger. People remember your involvement, environments show signs of your passage, and situations rarely close cleanly behind you. In scenes where past actions, unresolved consequences, or recognizable behavior patterns matter, the GM may treat you as already known, suspected, or associated with trouble, even if you were not formally identified at the time. This does not grant automatic failure, impose fixed penalties, or override successful disguises or preparations. Instead, it establishes that your presence carries narrative weight: doors may open with caution, questions may come earlier, and opportunities may arrive wrapped in expectation rather than trust. This reputation is not uniform. Some see you as capable. Others see you as dangerous. Many see you as both.
racial_human_act_before_it_settles_rules:
Humans move before certainty arrives. When danger escalates, plans fracture, or a situation shifts from controlled to volatile, Humans tend to act on instinct rather than waiting for clarity. This does not make them smarter than others. It makes them earlier.

Rules:
Once per session, when Initiative is about to be rolled or when a scene escalates from tension into open conflict, you may immediately take one Maneuver before the first Initiative order resolves. This Maneuver cannot be an Attack or Cast, but may be used to reposition, draw equipment, take cover, issue a warning, or interact with an unsecured object. This does not replace or delay your normal turn.
racial_human_fail_forward_rules-u:
Humans are not immune to failure. They simply refuse to let it be the end of the story. When something goes wrong, they improvise, adapt, and push the outcome sideways instead of stopping cold.

Rules:
Once per session, when you fail a skill check, you may choose to force partial progress instead of stalling. The attempt does not cleanly succeed; instead, it produces an unstable or compromised outcome that advances the situation while introducing immediate cost. You immediately suffer 1d4 Strain, and the result of the action is treated as incomplete, risky, or short-lived in a way that follows directly from how you pushed it through. This feature cannot be used on attack rolls, cannot negate consequences already resolved, and cannot turn failure into a clean success.
racial_human_stubborn_vitality_rules-u:
Humans keep functioning past the point where others would break. Pain, fear, shock, and exhaustion slow them down, but they do not stop them outright. What they ignore in the moment always comes due later.

Rules:
Once per session, when you would be prevented from taking an Action or Maneuver on your turn due to pain, shock, fear, exhaustion, or similar pressure short of Incapacitation, you may act anyway. You take your turn normally, ignoring that restriction for this turn only. At the end of your turn, you immediately suffer 1d6 Strain. This feature does not allow you to act if you are Incapacitated, unconscious, or otherwise completely unable to function, and it does not remove or negate any conditions affecting you.
racial_khadra_burden_of_the_oath_rules-u: If you voluntarily abandon or knowingly act against an oath you invoked during the current scene, the GM introduces a meaningful complication tied to trust, reputation, or spiritual unease. This complication creates lasting narrative friction—doors that were open may close, and allies may look at you with newfound caution. If circumstances beyond your control make the oath impossible to fulfill, this complication does not trigger.
racial_khadra_oathbound_bearing_rules-u: Once per session, when you openly invoke an oath, vow, or sworn responsibility, the GM may treat your stance as "weight-bearing" for the interaction. This can be used to establish seriousness in a negotiation, delay an escalation, or frame your position as being too costly for an opponent to challenge without serious consequences.
racial_khadra_stone_set_discipline_rules: Once per scene, when environmental pressure, crowd movement, or sudden disruption would force you to abandon a task, lose focus, or reposition against your will, you may choose to continue as intended. This does not prevent direct damage or grapples, but it allows you to resist interruptions caused by panic, confusion, or instability.
racial_khadra_unyielding_frame_rules-u: Once per session, when you would suffer a condition that disrupts your ability to act—such as Impaired or Shaken—you may ignore that condition entirely. The triggering effect still occurs narratively, and any damage or secondary consequences still apply, but you act normally on your next turn. This cannot negate effects that remove control entirely, such as paralysis or unconsciousness.
racial_kitsu_cyber_spirit_awareness_rule:
To a Kitsu, the world is a series of overlapping frequencies. You perceive the "static" of hidden surveillance, veiled technology, and residual magic as a physical pressure against your senses. You can feel when a space is being watched or when the digital reality has been intentionally thinned.

Rules: Once per session, you may ask the GM whether something in the current scene is deliberately hidden, concealed, or observing you. The GM must answer truthfully, but vaguely (e.g., "The air feels heavy with a lens's focus," or "There is a rhythmic ripple in the Veil here"). This feature does not identify sources, intentions, or methods.
racial_kitsu_foxfire_reflex_rules-u:
Your body often reacts before your conscious mind has finished processing the threat. This is not mere luck; it is a neurological and spiritual alignment that allows you to move with a sudden, flickering grace. Whether you are avoiding a kinetic round or a digital spike, you exist in a state of constant, coiled readiness.

Rules: Once per session, when you fail a Dodge or Coordination check, you may reroll the check and keep the new result. Alternatively, before rolling, you may spend 2 Strain to gain a bonus die on that check. You must declare this choice before the roll is resolved. This trait does not grant additional actions or movement beyond what the roll itself allows.
racial_kitsu_sacred_misdirection_rules-u:
In your culture, the "Mask" is a sacred tool of survival. By using your polyphonic voice to layer subtext and your body language to redirect attention, you can make even the most observant opponent misread your intent. To you, a clever lie is not a betrayal; it is the restoration of balance.

Rules: Once per session, when you fail a Charm, Deception, Stealth, or Disguise check involving trickery or social masking, you may reroll the check and keep the new result. This trait cannot be used for direct coercion, intimidation, or displays of brute-force authority.
racial_kitsu_the_impulse_for_motion_rule:
Kitsu have an instinctive aversion to stagnation. When a situation remains unresolved—when the conversation circles or the tension lingers without a catalyst—you feel a compulsive need to probe the boundaries. You are the "pebble that starts the landslide," forcing the world to reveal its hand.

Rules (Complication): When a scene remains static under high tension (prolonged negotiation, waiting under threat, etc.), the GM may offer you a tempting opportunity to interfere, escalate, or probe the situation. If accepted, the scene advances immediately but introduces a narrative complication. If declined, no penalty applies. This exists to create momentum when the group is stalled.
racial_lyranni_calculated_composure_rule:
Legacy lingers in how the Lyranni speak, move, and pause. Their composure carries weight in spaces shaped by tradition, ceremony, or ideological conflict. Whether invoking ancient etiquette or quietly defying it, they understand how ritual influences perception.

Rules:
Once per session, when you fail a Charm, Performance, or Etiquette check in a formal, ceremonial, or culturally significant context, you may reroll the check and keep the new result. This feature does not override active opposition, expose deception, or force compliance; it establishes composure and credibility rather than control.
racial_lyranni_legacy_logic_rules-u:
Lyranni minds are shaped by generations of arcane exposure. Patterns surface more easily. Symbols connect faster. Where others struggle to interpret magical residue or ritual logic, the Lyranni recognize familiar tension in unfamiliar forms.

Rules:
Once per session, after failing a roll to interpret, analyze, or recall information related to magic, ritual, or Veil phenomena, you may immediately reroll the check. You must take the new result. This feature does not apply to casting spells, resisting magical effects, or modifying spell outcomes.
racial_lyranni_veil_intuition_rules-u:
The Veil is not distant to the Lyranni. It presses close, felt as pressure, warmth, or unease long before it manifests openly. Even without spellcasting, most Lyranni learn to recognize when the world is no longer behaving naturally.

Rules:
Once per session, you may ask the GM whether arcane, spiritual, or Veil-based influence is present in the current scene. The GM must answer truthfully, but vaguely, indicating presence, intensity, or proximity only. This feature does not identify sources, spells, effects, intentions, or methods, and does not replace investigation or analysis.
racial_lyranni_veil_sensitivity_rules-u:
The Veil does not sit evenly around the Lyranni. Its shifts, fractures, and instabilities register in their bodies long before they become visible or disruptive. Where others experience a Veil event as a sudden intrusion, Lyranni often feel it approaching—like a change in pressure before a storm. How this sensitivity manifests varies from individual to individual. Some experience headaches, vertigo, or nosebleeds. Others feel nausea, heat behind the eyes, trembling hands, emotional unease, or a subtle distortion in perception. A few describe it as an ache in old scars, a ringing in the ears, or the sense that something nearby is out of alignment. Whatever the form, the sensation is unmistakable once learned.

Rules:
When the local Veil state is unstable, strained, thinning, or actively changing—such as near rifts, fractures, breaches, ritual disturbances, or heavily Veil-saturated environments—the GM may describe physical or sensory discomfort affecting you. This sensation does not identify the source, location, cause, or intent of the disturbance, and it never provides tactical detail or automatic warning. It exists to signal that the Veil is no longer behaving normally. Veil Sensitivity never causes automatic failure and does not impose fixed mechanical penalties by default. Instead, it creates persistent sensory pressure that may complicate concentration, rest, social composure, or prolonged exposure at the GM’s discretion. Appropriate preparation, distance, shielding, or removal from the affected area can reduce or eliminate these effects. This sensitivity is constant but uneven. Over time, most Lyranni learn to recognize their own tells—but they can never fully ignore them.
racial_veyra_light_sensitivity_rules-u:
The same sensory adaptations that make you a master of the dark make bright environments physically taxing. High-lumen industrial lighting or direct daylight overwhelms your reflective eyes, forcing you to compensate through the use of filters or by sticking to the shadows.

Rules: In brightly lit environments such as daylight or exposed white-lumen interiors, the GM may impose situational penalties on Perception checks that rely on sight unless you are using protective eyewear. This sensitivity never causes automatic failure and does not affect your Ultrasonic Awareness or other non-visual senses.
racial_veyra_mechanical_intuition_rules-:
A Veyra mind is naturally calibrated to identify patterns in circuitry and stress points in physical structures. You possess an instinctual grasp of how things are meant to function, allowing you to troubleshoot unfamiliar technology or improvised rigs with startling speed. You do not need a manual to understand the intent behind a design.

Rules: Once per scene, when interacting with an unfamiliar, damaged, or proprietary technological device, you may treat a Mechanics or Electronics check as plausible for understanding its current state and basic purpose. This establishes functional comprehension but does not grant automatic control or security clearance.
racial_veyra_prehensile_tail_rules-u:
The Veyra tail is a dense, muscular limb that functions as a high-precision stabilizer and a functional third point of contact. It serves as an essential counterbalance that allows the Veyra to operate in environments where other species would struggle to maintain their footing. In narrow ducts, on high-altitude ledges, or while suspended within vibrating maintenance shafts, the tail provides the necessary anchoring to work with both hands without the risk of a fall.

Rules: Your tail acts as a brace or hook to stabilize you while performing physical or technical tasks. When you are positioned in tight spaces, on narrow footing, or while partially suspended, you are treated as having secure anchoring for the purpose of maintaining your position. Limitations: The tail can hold or manipulate small, unattended objects and tools, but it cannot wield weapons or operate complex devices independently. It does not grant additional actions or attacks beyond your standard turn.
racial_veyra_ultrasonic_awareness_rules-:
Veyra do not rely on light alone to navigate. Through a series of subtle clicks and the interpretation of reflected vibrations, they build a persistent spatial map of their surroundings. This biological sonar allows them to operate with full confidence in total darkness, sensing the shape and movement of the world around them even when their large eyes cannot find a light source.

Rules: You can perceive creatures, obstacles, and movement within Short range in total darkness without penalties. This perception reveals shape, distance, and motion but does not provide fine detail, color, or text. This feature is suppressed if you are deafened or in an environment that actively dampens or distorts sound.
read_the_room_desc-u: Once per session, when entering a tense social or public situation, you may ask the GM one of the following questions: Who here is most dangerous right now? or What unspoken rule matters most in this space? The answer reflects immediate social reality rather than hidden secrets.
recording_kit_desc-u: Portable audio/video equipment, editing tools, and storage media suitable for fast turnaround work.
refuge_network_contact_desc-u: Knows safehouses, aid routes, and who still helps without asking questions.
research_go_bag_desc-u: Field notebooks (physical or digital), portable scanners, archival tools, reference chips, and basic preservation supplies.
response_loadout_desc-u: Tactical clothing, basic breach tools, and utility equipment retained or salvaged from service.
ritual_implements_desc-u: Symbols, tools, texts, or objects used in ceremonies, rites, or observances, some legal, some questionable.
salvage_stash_desc-u: A small cache of practical items gathered after the collapse: scavenged tools, emergency meds, a weapon-sized space, and supplies chosen for survival rather than comfort.
scavenger_guide_desc-u: An experienced pathfinder who knows which ruins are worth entering—and which ones kill the unprepared.
scrap_survival_kit_desc-u: Basic tools, repair tape, scavenged parts, water filtration, and emergency rations cobbled together from experience rather than manuals.
security_desc-u: Covers the understanding, bypassing, disabling, and construction of physical and digital security measures—including magitech locks, surveillance grids, biometric scanners, alarm wards, and defensive enchantments. Blends hardware and arcane knowledge, often used in tandem with Slicing, Electronics, or Archanotech during infiltration.
settlement_elder_or_organizer_desc-u: Someone who keeps a Fringe community functioning through favors, memory, and hard-earned trust.
shift_foreman_desc-u: Knows schedules, access points, and which corners are quietly ignored.
sleight_of_hand_desc-u: Covers the art of manual deception—pocketing items unnoticed, planting objects, palming cards or contraband, and manipulating attention through subtle hand movements. In Arcadia's surveillance-soaked streets, mastering this skill means knowing not just how to act, but when to misdirect. Often used in conjunction with Deception, Disguise, or Stealth.
slicing_desc-u: The art of digital infiltration—used to breach systems, override security, disable networks, and manipulate data in real time. Governs attacks on both civilian and corporate systems, drones, security protocols, and arcane-tech hybrids. Used for offensive and defensive hacking, system control, and interacting with ICE or node structures in ArcNet space.
smuggler_or_guide_desc-u: Specializes in moving people quietly through dangerous or restricted zones.
social_fixer_desc-u: A broker of introductions, favors, and quiet corrections when reputations are at risk.
social_gravity_desc-u: Once per session, when you initiate a social or institutional interaction from a position of assumed authority or legitimacy, gain +1 bonus die on the first roll of that exchange. This does not apply to threats, coercion, or criminal negotiations.
specialized_reference_cache_desc-u: A curated collection of restricted texts, encrypted files, or recovered academic records tied to your area of study.
spirit_lore_desc-u: Covers knowledge of spirits, soul-anchored entities, planar echoes, and other non-corporeal beings. Deals with the practical understanding of how spirits interact with the world, the rites used to summon or banish them, their temperaments, and their metaphysical laws. Used by mediums, summoners, wardens, and anyone who navigates the boundaries between life, death, and the unseen.
station_tech_or_dockmaster_desc-u: Knows how to bend port rules, delay inspections, or get access to restricted infrastructure.
stealth_desc-u: The practiced ability to move unseen and unheard—slipping through a guarded hallway, vanishing into urban crowds, or lying motionless while danger stalks past. Reflects control of breath, rhythm, posture, and situational awareness. In Arcadia, staying invisible means evading both human eyes and machine sensors.
street_medic_or_fence_desc-u: Someone who patches people up or moves goods quietly, no questions asked.
streetwise_desc-u: Measures your understanding of urban culture, criminal behavior, and the unspoken rules of the streets. The skill of knowing where to look, who to talk to, what not to say, and when to disappear. From dealing with black market contacts to reading gang tags or navigating underground enclaves, Streetwise helps you survive where law and order are suggestions.
survival_urban_desc-u: Measures your ability to endure and adapt to the hostile conditions of mega-city sprawls and ruins. Covers scavenging, rooftop travel, safehouses, gang turf and curfew navigation. Attempting urban survival techniques in other environments defaults to Hard or Extreme difficulty at GM discretion.
survival_veil_touched_desc-u: Measures your ability to endure contaminated or Veil-touched areas with bio, chem, and rad hazards and unstable physics. Covers decontamination and filter rotation, dosimeter and ward use, hot-zone route planning, anomaly mapping, setting safe anchors and null-tethers, reading echo-weather, identifying mutagenic flora and fauna, and triage for exposure.
survival_wilderness_desc-u: Measures your ability to endure and adapt in natural terrain—forests, mountains, plains. Covers natural hazards, identifying edible flora and fauna, weather craft, and shelter craft. Attempting wilderness survival in other environments defaults to Hard or Extreme difficulty at GM discretion.
swim_desc-u: Measures your ability to stay afloat, propel yourself through water, and survive in aquatic environments. Covers both casual movement through calm water and survival-grade swimming in combat, strong currents, or while encumbered. Also used when swimming in hazardous conditions—carrying gear, dragging allies, or escaping from submerged vehicles.
systems_toolkit_desc-u: Diagnostic tools, calibration equipment, spare components, and interface cables scavenged from Offworld infrastructure.
tag_assess_rules-u: Reveal, infer, or analyze information; gain insight without altering outcomes directly.
tag_assist_rules-u: Grant bonus dice or rerolls to another character.
tag_attack_rules-u: Makes or modifies an attack roll.
tag_aura_rules-u: Ongoing area effect centered on a creature or location.
tag_brace_rules-u: Resist forced movement, knockdown, disarm, or loss of control.
tag_burst_rules-u: Short-term spike in damage or output.
tag_concentration_rules-u: Ends if another Concentration effect begins.
tag_debuff_rules-u: Apply penalty dice or negative conditions (Impaired, Slowed, etc.).
tag_disable_rules-u: Remove or limit Actions, Maneuvers, Reactions, or system functions.
tag_enhance_rules-u: Improve rolls, stats, or effectiveness numerically.
tag_exploit_rules-u: Gains benefit from an existing debuff, failure, or revealed weakness.
tag_guard_rules-u: Reduce or negate damage to self or ally.
tag_interrupt_rules-u: Interfere with an action before it resolves.
tag_mitigation_rules-u: Flat reduction to damage or effects without full negation.
tag_possession_rules-u: Spirit or mind inhabits and directs a host.
tag_repair_rules-u: Restore HP or clear damage states (machines, constructs, frames).
tag_stance_rules-u: Temporary mode that alters how the character functions.
tag_summon_rules-u: Creates or calls an allied entity.
tag_transformation_rules-u: Temporarily alters form, capabilities, or body rules.
tailored_wardrobe_desc-u: High-quality clothing and accessories appropriate to elite environments, designed to project status and credibility.
talent_alteri_author_of_the_moment_rules: Type: Special Immediate (Narrative Control) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — During a sustained social interaction, declare one reasonable assumption about your role, presence, or relationship to the situation that has not yet been contradicted. For the remainder of the scene, neutral observers treat that assumption as true unless actively challenged or disproven. You gain +1 bonus die on the first roll made to act in accordance with that assumption.
talent_alteri_behavioral_study_rules-u: Type: Action (Observation) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — After a brief interaction or focused observation of a specific individual, you may attempt to prepare a Mask based on them. Make an Insight roll. On a Success, you may finalize the Mask without requiring a full scene of observation. If the subject is alert, guarded, or trained to resist scrutiny, this roll is opposed by their Willpower. On a Failure, a full scene of observation is still required.
talent_alteri_biometric_plausibility_rul: Type: Passive (Impersonation Systems) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When impersonating a specific individual or role, you may use Performance (Impersonation) in place of Slicing or Electronics to spoof non-hardened biometric systems such as facial recognition, voiceprint locks, gait analysis, or behavioral authentication. You gain +1 bonus die on Performance (Impersonation) rolls made to bypass such systems. This does not apply to hardened or multi-factor biometric security.
talent_alteri_compressed_observation_rul: Type: Passive (Study Acceleration) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — A brief interaction or short period of focused observation is always sufficient to attempt preparation of a Mask. On a Success, the Mask may be finalized immediately. On a Hard Success, the Mask does not suffer unfamiliarity penalties during its first scene of use.
talent_alteri_disciplined_morphology_rul: Type: Passive (Physiology) • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Through practice and internal control, you can extend your morphic limits beyond standard Alteri capability. When assuming a Mask, you may convincingly impersonate humanoid targets that are significantly larger or smaller than your natural form by altering tissue density, posture, and internal structure. This expands your plausible appearance range beyond your base size but does not change your Size stat, reach, damage, encumbrance, or physical capabilities.
talent_alteri_hardened_biometric_assimil: Type: Free Action (Persona Clone) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Flawlessly replicate a target’s full persona (appearance, mannerisms, tone); gain +1 bonus die to Disguise, Performance, or Deception checks tied to that impersonation.
talent_alteri_identity_saturation_rules-: Type: Passive (Cognitive Integration) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you first activate a prepared Mask in a scene, your instincts and habitual responses immediately align with that identity. Once per scene, you may treat one failed Performance (Impersonation), Deception, or Etiquette roll made while operating under that Mask as a Success instead of a Failure.
talent_alteri_immaculate_impersonation_r: Type: Passive (Identity Authority) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you activate a prepared Mask of a specific individual, neutral and friendly observers and non-hostile systems accept you as the legitimate individual for the duration of the scene unless presented with direct, compelling contradiction or incontrovertible proof. You gain +1 bonus die on Performance (Impersonation), Deception, and Etiquette rolls made to sustain this identity. This does not override prior personal familiarity or systems designed to verify genetic identity.
talent_alteri_institutional_passing_rule: Type: Passive (Systemic Plausibility) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — While operating under a prepared Mask, routine institutional interactions such as basic credential checks, informal questioning, and low-level access control treat you as legitimate unless the system or personnel are actively alerted, hostile, or operating under heightened security. This does not bypass formal verification, insider recognition, or systems designed to detect altered physiology.
talent_alteri_intent_mirroring_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Behavioral Echo) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When a character directs a clear social action toward you, such as asserting authority, probing familiarity, or applying pressure, you may mirror their intent through matching posture, cadence, and demeanor. Gain +1 bonus die to your next Insight, Deception, Persuade, or Performance (Impersonation) roll made directly in response.
talent_alteri_layered_identity_rules-u: Type: Passive (Identity Capacity) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — You may maintain two prepared Masks simultaneously. Switching between prepared Masks during an active scene requires an Action and costs 1d4 Strain. Outside of an active scene, switching between prepared Masks does not cost Strain. Only one Mask may be socially active at a time.
talent_alteri_living_mask_archive_rules-: Type: Passive (Identity Capacity) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will / 1/Session — You may maintain up to four prepared Masks simultaneously. Outside of active scenes, you may switch freely between prepared Masks without cost. Once per session, you may switch to any prepared Mask during a scene without spending an Action or Strain, provided the transition is plausibly supported by the environment or social context.
talent_alteri_perfect_adaptation_rules-u: Type: Passive (Behavioral Mastery) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you enter a scene under a prepared Mask, choose one social domain relevant to that identity, such as authority, familiarity, subservience, professionalism, or anonymity. For the rest of the scene, you are treated as naturally fluent in that domain. Once during the scene, you may treat one failed Performance (Impersonation), Deception, Persuade, or Etiquette roll tied to that domain as a Success instead of a Failure.
talent_alteri_plausible_existence_rules-: Type: Passive (Assumption) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — While acting within the expected role, behavior, and social position of your current Mask, neutral observers and non-alert systems treat your presence as legitimate by default unless they possess a concrete reason to doubt you. This does not override active investigations, formal verification, direct contradictions, or known hostile intelligence.
talent_alteri_predictive_empathy_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Behavioral Forecast) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose a character you are directly interacting with and make an Insight roll. On a Success, you may determine that character’s most likely behavioral approach in the next exchange, such as pressing, withdrawing, testing authority, seeking reassurance, or attempting deception. On a Hard Success, you also gain +1 bonus die to the first roll you make that directly exploits this expectation.
talent_alteri_seamless_transition_rules-: Type: Special Immediate (Identity Shift) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — You may switch to a prepared Mask without consuming an Action, provided the transition is supported by plausible movement, environmental cover, or social interruption. This does not prevent scrutiny if the transition itself would be implausible within the situation.
talent_alteri_second_skin_rules-u: Type: Passive (Identity) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — You may maintain one additional Mask beyond your default identity. Switching Masks requires an Action and costs 1d4 Strain when performed during an active scene or situation. Outside of an active scene, switching Masks does not cost Strain.
talent_alteri_social_attunement_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Assessment) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose a character you are directly interacting with and make an Insight roll. On a Success, you learn the general social expectations they have of you in the interaction, such as authority, familiarity, distance, or formality. On a Hard Success, you also learn one behavioral adjustment that would improve alignment with those expectations.
talent_alteri_social_blind_spot_rules-u: Type: Passive (Cognitive Absence) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — While operating under a prepared Mask in a contextually appropriate setting, casual observers do not form strong or lasting impressions of you. Minor inconsistencies or forgettable details do not escalate scrutiny unless reinforced by direct contradiction, repeated engagement, or external evidence. This does not affect targeted surveillance, dedicated tracking, or characters already actively focused on you.
talent_alteri_social_null_field_rules-u: Type: Passive (Cognitive Absence) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — For the duration of a scene, your presence does not anchor attention or memory unless you take decisive or disruptive action. Casual observers struggle to recall your appearance, voice, or specific involvement once focus moves elsewhere. This does not affect targeted surveillance, active tracking, or characters already focused on you.
talent_draevi_body_like_the_road_rules-u: Type: Passive (Physiological Adaptation) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Gain +1 bonus die on CON rolls to resist poison, disease, spoiled food, and environmental toxins. If you fail such a roll, reduce any ongoing non-magical effect by one step in duration or severity. This does not negate magical poisons or supernatural afflictions.
talent_draevi_bound_to_the_old_paths_rul: Type: Special Immediate (Enduring Passage) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you lead a group through sustained movement, escape, or travel under pressure, affirm the route and press on. One failed Survival, Navigation, or movement-related roll made by you or an ally as part of that sequence is treated as a success, reflecting inherited route knowledge and practiced continuity rather than improvisation or speed.
talent_draevi_circle_of_the_standing_sto: Type: Action (Ancestral Perimeter) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — You establish a protective perimeter within Medium range centered on your current position by marking space through repetition, placement, and practiced movement tied to ancestral memory. While you remain within the perimeter, you and allies inside it gain +1 bonus die on rolls made to resist forced movement, disorientation, or environmental hazards. If you leave the perimeter, the effect immediately dissipates.
talent_draevi_claim_the_ground_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Anchoring Stance) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you deliberately take position at the start of a scene or after a brief pause, designate your location as claimed ground. Until you leave it, you gain +1 bonus die on rolls to resist forced movement, knockdown, or environmental disruption. Allies within Short range gain +1 bonus die on the first roll they make to brace, stabilize, or reposition in that space.
talent_draevi_echoes_beneath_the_stone_r: Type: Free Action (Ancestral Guidance) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you pause in a location shaped by long use, travel, loss, or shared hardship, roll Spirit Lore. On a success, you receive symbolic impressions tied to what the place remembers. Your next roll made this scene that follows that guidance gains +1 bonus die.
talent_draevi_endure_the_long_walk_rules: Type: Special Immediate (Deferred Strain) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you would gain Strain, fatigue, or an environmental condition due to exposure, deprivation, weather, or sustained exertion, you may delay that effect until the end of the current scene. The effect is not negated and applies in full once the scene ends.
talent_draevi_eyes_of_the_pathkeeper_rul: Type: Free Action (Situational Read) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you pause to observe a physical space rather than act immediately, roll Perception or Survival. On a success, choose one: identify the safest route through the area, determine the most stable position to hold, or recognize signs of prior passage, habitation, or danger. Your next roll directly tied to that choice gains +1 bonus die.
talent_draevi_ground_remembers_me_rules-: Type: Free Action (Place Familiarity) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you remain in a location and act with deliberate presence, that space becomes familiar ground for the rest of the scene. While within it, you and allies within Short range gain +1 bonus die on rolls made to navigate, brace, hide, or reposition using the environment. The effect ends if the location is abandoned or radically altered.
talent_draevi_keeper_of_the_last_fire_ru: Type: Free Action (Sanctuary) • Cost: — • Usage: Resets after each Long Rest — When you establish a short or long rest in a location you have personally secured, sheltered, or deliberately chosen, you draw on shared custom and hard-earned routine to keep the group steady. You and one ally of your choice each recover 1d6 Strain at the end of that rest. In addition, during the rest, all First Aid and Medicine checks made to tend wounds or assist recovery for you or that ally gain +1 bonus die.
talent_draevi_lessons_carved_in_flesh_ru: Type: Passive (Adaptive Memory) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you face an environmental hazard, travel condition, or survival challenge you have previously endured during play, you gain +1 bonus die on the first roll you make to overcome it. If the situation would normally impose a penalty die, this bonus die instead cancels one such penalty.
talent_draevi_marks_of_the_road_rules-u: Type: Passive (Lived Memory) • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Choose one skill: Survival, Track, or Spirit Lore. When recalling routes, camps, hazards, land-bound rites, or lessons learned through travel or hardship, you gain +1 bonus die on checks using the chosen skill when lived experience is relevant.
talent_draevi_read_the_ground_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Pattern Recognition) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When entering or crossing a physical environment with coherent terrain or crowd flow, roll Survival or Perception. On a success, you gain +1 bonus die on your next roll this scene made to move through, reposition within, or brace against that environment. This does not detect threats or intentions.
talent_draevi_shared_burden_shared_breat: Type: Special Immediate (Endurance Aid) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When an ally within Short range fails a CON, Athletics, or Survival roll to endure fatigue, exposure, or environmental hardship, they may immediately reroll the check and take the second result. If the reroll succeeds, you regain 1 Strain as the shared effort steadies you rather than exhausting you.
talent_draevi_stand_when_others_stagger_: Type: Reaction (Endurance Check) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you fail a CON or Athletics roll made to resist fatigue, environmental stress, knockdown, or forced repositioning, you may immediately reroll the check and take the second result. This reroll cannot be further modified.
talent_draevi_stonewise_bearing_rules-u: Type: Passive (Environmental Literacy) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — You treat difficult, unstable, or uneven physical terrain as manageable for the purpose of movement and positioning. You do not suffer penalty dice or additional movement costs from rubble, loose ground, broken infrastructure, or natural hazards when repositioning deliberately. This does not increase speed, negate forced movement, or prevent falls caused by external force.
talent_draevi_those_who_walked_before_ru: Type: Special Immediate (Shared Memory) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When an ally within Short range hesitates, falters, or fails a roll tied to judgment, morale, or decisive action, they may immediately reroll the check and take the second result. This cannot be used on attack rolls.
talent_draevi_unbroken_path_rules-u: Type: Special Immediate (Continuity Assertion) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When a coordinated movement, journey, or escape would fail due to confusion, obstruction, or loss of direction, one failed Navigation, Survival, or positioning-related roll made as part of that effort is treated as a success. This represents practiced continuity rather than improvisation.
talent_draevi_voice_of_those_who_remain_: Type: Free Action (Rally of Purpose) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you state a clear, shared objective during a tense scene, choose up to three allies within Short range who can hear you. The first roll each chosen ally makes that directly advances that stated objective gains +1 bonus die. This effect lasts until used or until the scene ends.
talent_draevi_weight_of_presence_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Stabilizing Authority) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When chaos, panic, or instability threatens to scatter a group in a physical space, allies within Short range who can see you gain +1 bonus die on rolls to resist fear, disorientation, or rash action until the start of your next turn.
talent_draevi_whispers_of_what_was_rules: Type: Action (Ancestral Vision) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you pause in a location shaped by past movement, conflict, loss, or shared use, you still yourself and listen for what the place remembers. You receive fragmented, symbolic visions of events that occurred there in the past, shown as impressions, images, or moments without narration or explanation. These visions never reveal the future and never answer questions directly. After receiving the vision, you gain +1 bonus die on your next roll made this scene to investigate, interpret, or contextualize the location or revealed events using Spirit Lore, History, Survival, or Occult Lore. This bonus applies only to understanding and interpretation, not to direct action or combat.
talent_feran_anglecraft_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Situational Read) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you enter a space where movement, elevation, or sightlines meaningfully shape risk—such as alleys, rooftops, scaffolded structures, transit platforms, ruins, or dense interiors—roll Perception or an appropriate Survival skill, depending on the environment. On a success, you gain +1 bonus die on your next Dodge, positioning, or escape-related roll made in that space this scene. This represents reading angles, terrain flow, and commitment rather than detecting specific threats or identifying solutions.
talent_feran_chaos_has_a_line_rules-u: Type: Action (Predatory Focus) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When operating in a physically disordered scene, choose one visible creature, location, or exit that you intend to reach, pursue, or break through. Until the end of the scene, you gain +1 bonus die on rolls made to reposition under pressure, attack, Dodge, or perform physical skill actions that directly advance that stated goal. If you abandon that goal or redirect to an unrelated objective, this effect immediately ends.
talent_feran_chaos_is_a_map_rules-u: Type: Passive (Interpretive Authority) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will - When a physical scene becomes disordered through crowd panic, structural damage, overlapping movement, or competing threats, you may treat that disorder as navigable rather than obstructive. You may attempt rolls to choose routes, timing, or positioning using Perception, Survival, or Track even if no clear path or stable option exists. This does not grant bonuses, negate rolls, or ensure safety; it establishes that Feran can act meaningfully where others would stall due to confusion.
talent_feran_clean_footing_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Movement Recovery) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you fail a roll made to climb, leap, balance, or traverse unstable or hazardous terrain, you may immediately reroll the check and take the second result. This reroll may not be further modified. If the reroll fails, consequences apply normally.
talent_feran_finish_it_rules-u: Type: Action (Apex Commitment) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose one visible hostile creature and commit to ending the threat. Until the end of the scene, you gain +1 bonus die on attacks, Dodges, and physical skill rolls made directly against that creature or to pursue, corner, or prevent it from escaping. You cannot willingly disengage from that creature or redirect your focus to other enemies. If the creature escapes the scene, this effect ends.
talent_feran_ive_got_you_rules-u: Type: Action (Pack Commitment) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose one ally you can perceive and commit to watching their back. Until the end of the scene, you gain +1 bonus die on rolls made to reposition under pressure, Dodge, attack, or perform physical skill actions that directly protect that ally, relieve pressure on them, or prevent threats from reaching them. If you abandon that ally or act toward an unrelated objective, this effect immediately ends.
talent_feran_lines_between_us_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Pack Synchrony) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When an ally within Short range repositions, disengages, or breaks from cover under pressure, you may immediately adjust your own position to remain within Short range of them, provided the movement is physically plausible. This movement follows normal rules, may trigger reactions, and does not grant attacks. It represents instinctive pack movement rather than command or coordination.
talent_feran_low_light_focus_rules-u: Type: Passive (Sensory Discipline) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When operating in low-light, obscured, or shadowed conditions where visibility is impaired but not completely absent, you may negate one penalty die imposed on Perception, Survival, or positioning-related rolls caused by lighting alone. This does not grant vision in total darkness, does not negate penalties from smoke, visual interference, or concealment, and does not apply to tasks requiring fine visual detail. It reflects trained focus and motion-reading rather than enhanced sight.
talent_feran_measured_chase_rules-u: Type: Special Immediate (Pursuit Discipline) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When your Instinct to Pursue ancestry feature triggers and you choose to pursue, you gain +1 bonus die on your next Athletics, Coordination, or Track roll made this scene to maintain contact, cut distance, or intercept the fleeing target. In addition, the complication introduced by Instinct to Pursue must be immediate and concrete rather than open-ended, reflecting controlled pressure instead of reckless overextension. This does not negate escalation or consequences.
talent_feran_path_sense_rules-u: Type: Passive (Environmental Tracking) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will - When attempting to track, follow, or reconstruct the movement of a creature, vehicle, or group through a physical environment, you gain +1 bonus die on Track or appropriate Survival rolls so long as the path involved movement, disturbance, or repeated passage. This applies even in dense urban spaces, vertical zones, or partially obscured terrain. This does not reveal destinations or intent; it improves your ability to stay on the trail once movement has occurred.
talent_feran_run_it_down_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Predatory Override) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you would gain one or more of the following conditions: Hobbled, Slowed, Impaired, Stunned, Suppressed, Entangled/Restrained, or Panicked, you may instead push through them. Until the end of the scene, you ignore the effects of those conditions and may reposition under pressure, attack, Dodge, and take physical actions normally. Damage and all other consequences still apply. When the scene ends, your Strain is immediately reduced to 0 and you gain the Exhausted condition.
talent_feran_scent_of_disruption_rules-u: Type: Passive (Multisensory Tracking) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will - When following a trail, reconstructing movement, or assessing recent activity, you may use Perception or Survival even when visibility is severely limited by darkness, smoke, rain, crowd density, or visual interference. In such conditions, penalties imposed specifically by obscured vision do not apply. This does not reveal concealed or stationary targets and does not function in environments without meaningful physical cues.
talent_feran_sense_the_shift_rules-u: Type: Action (Flow Prediction) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session - You deliberately observe and listen for changes in spatial rhythm, pressure, and movement. Roll Perception, Investigation , or an appropriate Survival skill depending on the environment. On a success, choose one: an opening in movement or access, a change in crowd or patrol behavior, or an impending structural or environmental reaction. You gain +1 bonus die on your next roll this scene made to exploit, avoid, or respond to that predicted shift. This does not reveal causes, identities, or outcomes, and does not prevent consequences if you misjudge the moment.
talent_feran_shared_blind_instinct_rules: Type: Reaction (Pack Reflex) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session - When you and at least one ally within Short range are targeted by the same threat, hazard, or area effect that relies on positioning or movement, you may allow one affected ally to use your result for a Dodge or positioning-related roll instead of their own. This represents instinctive synchronization born of trust rather than protection, command, or planning. This does not negate the effect, grant immunity, or allow multiple allies to copy the result.
talent_feran_silent_hand_off_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Instinctive Support) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session - When an ally within Short range is about to make a Dodge roll against a threat that you can also perceive, you may grant that ally +1 bonus die on the roll. You must declare this before the Dodge roll is made. This represents Feran danger-sense—subtle shifts in posture, timing, or spacing that help an ally move at exactly the right moment. This does not grant additional movement, negate the attack, or replace the ally’s action.
talent_feran_somethings_off_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Pattern Disruption Read) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene - When you pause briefly to observe a physical space rather than act immediately, roll Perception or Investigation . On a success, you identify one place where the natural flow of movement, sound, or use has been disrupted, such as an unusual pause, altered route, forced climb, or hurried exit. Your next roll made this scene to investigate, follow, or exploit that disruption gains +1 bonus die . This does not identify who caused it or why.
talent_feran_the_second_route_rules-u: Type: Action (Spatial Reframing) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session - When a route, plan, or approach becomes blocked, compromised, or dangerous, you may declare an alternate physical route that plausibly exists within the environment, such as an upper passage, maintenance access, crowd gap, structural bypass, or indirect path. The GM must allow this route to exist if it is physically reasonable. Using it still requires movement, rolls, and carries risk; this talent asserts Feran authority over finding viable paths others overlook.
talent_feran_there_is_still_a_way_rules-: Type: Special (Spatial Continuity) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When a physical environment would deny you all viable movement or positioning options due to collapse, blockage, crowd pressure, or extreme instability, you may assert a single physically plausible path or position that keeps you active in the scene. You immediately take it, resolving any required rolls normally. This does not negate risk or consequences; it ensures the scene cannot force you into inaction, stalling, or automatic retreat.
talent_feran_unclaimed_ground_rules-u: Type: Passive (Spatial Opportunity) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will - When you enter a contested or chaotic space, areas of partial cover, elevation, or indirect approach that others treat as unsafe or inefficient are considered viable positioning options for you if they are physically plausible. The GM should treat such spaces as usable without requiring additional justification. This does not negate risk, negate rolls, or guarantee safety; it establishes Feran comfort in marginal ground.
talent_feran_where_the_floor_gives_way_r: Type: Reaction (Environmental Foresight) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session - When an environment is about to respond violently to stress or movement, such as collapse, crowd surge, structural failure, or sudden instability, you may act just before that reaction resolves. You may reposition yourself or one ally within Short range to safer footing or partial cover. This does not negate the event, prevent damage entirely, or guarantee safety; it allows Feran to move on the beat before failure.
